revision.comment: Sujikai がページ「[[信夫三山暁まいり]]」を「[[信夫三山暁参り大わらじ奉納]]」に移動しました: 以前の記事名に戻す
revision.text: #転送 [[信夫三山暁参り大わらじ奉納]]

'''信夫三山暁まいり'''（しのぶさんざんあかつきまいり）とは旧正月14日にあたる毎年2月10日から11日に[[福島県]][[福島市]]中心部にある[[信夫山]]で行われる[[祭|祭事]]。
==概要== 福島市市街地に隣接する信夫山。その信夫山を構成する羽山、羽黒山、熊野山の三峰を祀る[[山岳信仰]]であり400年近くの歴史を有する祭事である。日本一の大わらじを奉納する独自性から福島県を代表する祭事のひとつとして知られている。
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.f-kankou.jp/cgi-bin/f-kankou/asobu/page.cgi?id=70 信夫三山暁まいり] - 福島市観光コンベンション協会 *[https://www.ntt-east.co.jp/fukushima/fbrari/031/05.html イベント「信夫三山暁まいり」] - NTT東日本福島支店
{{DEFAULTSORT:しのふやまさんさんあかつきまいり}} [[Category:福島県の祭り]] [[Category:福島市]]

'''信夫三山暁まいり'''（しのぶさんざんあかつきまいり）とは旧正月14日にあたる毎年[[2月10日]]から[[2月11日|11日]]に[[福島県]][[福島市]]中心部にある[[信夫山]]で行われる[[祭|祭事]]。 {{seealso|福島わらじまつり}} == 概要 == 福島市市街地に隣接する信夫山。その信夫山を構成する羽山、羽黒山、熊野山の三峰を祀る[[山岳信仰]]であり400年近くの歴史を有する祭事である。日本一の大わらじを奉納する独自性から福島県を代表する祭事のひとつとして知られている。
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.f-kankou.jp/cgi-bin/f-kankou/asobu/page.cgi?id=70 信夫三山暁まいり] - 福島市観光コンベンション協会 * [https://www.ntt-east.co.jp/fukushima/fbrari/031/05.html イベント「信夫三山暁まいり」] - NTT東日本福島支店
{{DEFAULTSORT:しのふやまさんさんあかつきまいり}} [[Category:福島県の祭り]] [[Category:福島市]] [[Category:冬のイベント]]

{{otheruses|冬季2月の旧正月に行われる神事|夏季8月に催される祭事|福島わらじまつり}} '''信夫三山暁まいり'''（しのぶさんざんあかつきまいり）とは旧正月14日にあたる毎年[[2月10日]]から[[2月11日|11日]]に[[福島県]][[福島市]]中心部にある[[信夫山]]で行われる[[神事]]。 {{seealso|福島わらじまつり}} == 概要 == 福島市市街地に隣接する信夫山。その信夫山を構成する羽山、羽黒山、熊野山の三峰を祀る[[山岳信仰]]であり400年近くの歴史を有する祭事である。日本一の大わらじを奉納する独自性から福島県を代表する祭事のひとつとして知られている。
主なルートは信夫山の北嶺に位置する御山地区をスタートし、福島駅東口から福島市の中心市街地を経て、信夫山南峰から信夫山に入山。 頂上の羽黒神社まで奉納する。
== イベント == 同日10日に『暁まいり福男福女競走』が催され、暁まいりで巡行する道筋に準ずるルートを競い合う。
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.f-kankou.jp/cgi-bin/f-kankou/asobu/page.cgi?id=70 信夫三山暁まいり] - 福島市観光コンベンション協会 * [https://www.ntt-east.co.jp/fukushima/fbrari/031/05.html イベント「信夫三山暁まいり」] - NTT東日本福島支店
{{DEFAULTSORT:しのふやまさんさんあかつきまいり}} [[Category:福島県の祭り]] [[Category:福島市]] [[Category:冬のイベント]]

{{otheruses|冬季2月の旧正月に行われる神事|夏季8月に催される祭事|福島わらじまつり}} '''信夫三山暁まいり'''（しのぶさんざんあかつきまいり）とは旧正月14日にあたる毎年[[2月10日]]から[[2月11日|11日]]に[[福島県]][[福島市]]中心部にある[[信夫山]]で行われる[[神事]]。 {{seealso|福島わらじまつり}} == 概要 == 福島市市街地に隣接する信夫山。その信夫山を構成する羽山、羽黒山、熊野山の三峰を祀る[[山岳信仰]]であり400年近くの歴史を有する祭事である。日本一の大わらじを奉納する独自性から福島県を代表する祭事のひとつとして知られている。
主なルートは信夫山の北嶺に位置する御山地区をスタートし、福島駅東口から福島市の中心市街地を経て、信夫山南峰から信夫山に入山。 頂上の羽黒神社まで奉納する。
メディアでの扱いとして主だったものは、毎年地元[[コミュニティ放送|コミュニティラジオ]]の『[[福島コミュニティ放送|FMポコ]]』で暁まいりの実況生放送を配信している（ネット配信による全国無料視聴可能）。
== イベント == 同日10日に『暁まいり福男福女競走』が催され、暁まいりで巡行する道筋に準ずるルートを競い合う。

{{otheruses|冬季2月の旧正月に行われる神事|夏季8月に催される祭事|福島わらじまつり}} '''信夫三山暁まいり'''（しのぶさんざんあかつきまいり）とは旧正月14日にあたる毎年[[2月10日]]から[[2月11日|11日]]に[[福島県]][[福島市]]中心部にある[[信夫山]]で行われる[[神事]]。 {{seealso|福島わらじまつり}} == 概要 == 福島市市街地に隣接する信夫山。その信夫山を構成する羽山、羽黒山、熊野山の三峰を祀る[[山岳信仰]]であり400年近くの歴史を有する祭事である。日本一の大わらじを奉納する独自性から福島県を代表する祭事のひとつとして知られている。担ぎ手は地元有志の御山敬神会、地元商工会議所が参加しており、子供用のわらじの担ぎ手に地元小学生が参加している。

主なルートは信夫山の北峰に位置する御山地区をスタートし、反時計回りに福島駅東口から福島市の中心市街地を経て、信夫山南峰から信夫山に入山。 頂上の羽黒神社まで奉納する。
メディアでの扱いとして主だったものは、毎年地元[[コミュニティ放送|コミュニティラジオ]]の『[[福島コミュニティ放送|FMポコ]]』で暁まいりの実況生放送を配信している（ネット配信による全国無料視聴可能）。

== アクセス == 開催日当日は、臨時の無料駐車場が信夫山南峰の各所に設けられる

{{DEFAULTSORT:しのふやまさんさんあかつきまいり}} [[Category:福島県の祭り]] [[Category:福島市]] [[Category:冬のイベント]]

{{otheruses|冬季2月の旧正月に行われる神事|夏季8月に催される祭事|福島わらじまつり}} '''信夫三山暁まいり'''（しのぶさんざんあかつきまいり）とは旧正月14日にあたる毎年[[2月10日]]から[[2月11日|11日]]に[[福島県]][[福島市]]中心部にある[[信夫山]]で行われる[[神事]]。 {{seealso|福島わらじまつり}} == 概要 == 福島市市街地に隣接する信夫山。その信夫山を構成する羽山、羽黒山、熊野山の三峰を祀る[[山岳信仰]]であり400年近くの歴史を有する祭事である。日本一の大わらじを奉納する独自性から福島県を代表する祭事のひとつとして知られている。担ぎ手は地元有志の御山敬神会、地元商工会議所が参加しており、子供用のわらじの担ぎ手に地元小学生が参加している。
主なルートは信夫山の北峰に位置する御山地区をスタートし、反時計回りに福島駅東口から福島市の中心市街地を経て、信夫山南峰から信夫山に入山。 頂上の羽黒神社まで奉納する。
メディアでの扱いとして主だったものは、毎年地元[[コミュニティ放送|コミュニティラジオ]]の『[[福島コミュニティ放送|FMポコ]]』で暁まいりの実況生放送を配信している（ネット配信による全国無料視聴可能）。
== 大わらじの巡行ルート == 御山作業所([[国道13号]]沿い) → [[佐藤工業 (福島市)|佐藤工業本社]]([[福島県道3号福島飯坂線|飯坂街道]]沿い) → 福島成田山不動院 → [[曽根田駅]]・[[曽根田ショッピングセンター|MAXふくしま]] → [[福島駅 (福島県)|福島駅]]東口→福島市金融街(レンガ通り) → [[福島稲荷神社]](旧福島城下町地区氏神) → パセオ通り商店街 → 福島消防署 → 信夫山入山 → 黒沼神社(旧小山荒井村地区氏神)・[[福島縣護國神社]] → 信夫山ガイドセンター → 羽黒神社(頂上)
== イベント == 同日10日に『暁まいり福男福女競走』が催され、暁まいりで巡行する道筋に準ずるルートを競い合う。
== アクセス == 開催日当日は、臨時の無料駐車場が信夫山南峰の各所に設けられる
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.f-kankou.jp/cgi-bin/f-kankou/asobu/page.cgi?id=70 信夫三山暁まいり] - 福島市観光コンベンション協会 * [https://www.ntt-east.co.jp/fukushima/fbrari/031/05.html イベント「信夫三山暁まいり」] - NTT東日本福島支店
{{DEFAULTSORT:しのふやまさんさんあかつきまいり}} [[Category:福島県の祭り]] [[Category:福島市]] [[Category:冬のイベント]]

{{otheruses|冬季2月の旧正月に行われる神事|夏季8月に催される祭事|福島わらじまつり}} '''信夫三山暁まいり'''（しのぶさんざんあかつきまいり）とは旧正月14日にあたる毎年[[2月10日]]から[[2月11日|11日]]に[[福島県]][[福島市]]中心部にある[[信夫山]]で行われる[[神事]]。 {{seealso|福島わらじまつり}} == 概要 == 福島市市街地に隣接する信夫山。その信夫山を構成する羽山、羽黒山、熊野山の三峰を祀る[[山岳信仰]]であり400年近くの歴史を有する祭事である。日本一の大わらじを奉納する独自性から福島県を代表する祭事のひとつとして知られている。担ぎ手は地元有志の御山敬神会、地元商工会議所が参加しており、子供用のわらじの担ぎ手に地元小学生が参加している。
主なルートは信夫山の北峰に位置する御山地区をスタートし、反時計回りに福島駅東口から福島市の中心市街地を経て、信夫山南峰から信夫山に入山。 頂上の羽黒神社まで奉納する。
メディアでの扱いとして主だったものは、毎年地元[[コミュニティ放送|コミュニティラジオ]]の『[[福島コミュニティ放送|FMポコ]]』で暁まいりの実況生放送を配信している（ネット配信による全国無料視聴可能）。
== 大わらじの巡行ルート == 御山作業所([[国道13号]]沿い) → [[佐藤工業 (福島市)|佐藤工業本社]]([[福島県道3号福島飯坂線|飯坂街道]]沿い) → [[福島成田山不動院]] → [[曽根田駅]]・[[曽根田ショッピングセンター|MAXふくしま]] → [[福島駅 (福島県)|福島駅]]東口 → 福島市金融街(レンガ通り) → [[福島稲荷神社]](旧福島城下町地区氏神) → パセオ通り商店街 → 福島消防署 → 信夫山入山 → 黒沼神社(旧[[小山荒井村]]地区氏神)・[[福島縣護國神社]] → 信夫山ガイドセンター → [[羽黒神社]](頂上)
== イベント == 同日10日に『暁まいり福男福女競走』が催され、暁まいりで巡行する道筋に準ずるルートを競い合う。
== アクセス == 開催日当日は、臨時の無料駐車場が信夫山南峰の各所に設けられる
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.f-kankou.jp/cgi-bin/f-kankou/asobu/page.cgi?id=70 信夫三山暁まいり] - 福島市観光コンベンション協会 * [https://www.ntt-east.co.jp/fukushima/fbrari/031/05.html イベント「信夫三山暁まいり」] - NTT東日本福島支店
{{DEFAULTSORT:しのふやまさんさんあかつきまいり}} [[Category:福島県の祭り]] [[Category:福島市]] [[Category:冬のイベント]]

{{otheruses|冬季2月の旧正月に行われる神事|夏季8月に催される祭事|福島わらじまつり}} '''信夫三山暁まいり'''（しのぶさんざんあかつきまいり）とは旧正月14日にあたる毎年[[2月10日]]から[[2月11日|11日]]に[[福島県]][[福島市]]中心部にある[[信夫山]]で行われる[[神事]]。 {{seealso|福島わらじまつり}} == 概要 == 福島市市街地に隣接する信夫山。その信夫山を構成する羽山、羽黒山、熊野山の三峰を祀る[[山岳信仰]]であり400年近くの歴史を有する祭事である。日本一の大わらじを奉納する独自性から福島県を代表する祭事のひとつとして知られている。担ぎ手は地元有志の御山敬神会、地元商工会議所が参加しており、子供用のわらじの担ぎ手に地元小学生が参加している。

メディアでの扱いとして主だったものは、毎年地元[[コミュニティ放送|コミュニティラジオ]]の『[[福島コミュニティ放送|FMポコ]]』で暁まいりの実況生放送を配信している（ネット配信による全国無料視聴可能）。
長さ12m、幅1,2ｍ、重さ約2tという巨大なわらじを運ぶ。
== 大わらじの巡行ルート == 御山作業所([[国道13号]]沿い) → [[佐藤工業 (福島市)|佐藤工業本社]]([[福島県道3号福島飯坂線|飯坂街道]]沿い) → [[福島成田山不動院]] → [[曽根田駅]]・[[曽根田ショッピングセンター|MAXふくしま]] → [[福島駅 (福島県)|福島駅]]東口 → 福島市金融街(レンガ通り) → [[福島稲荷神社]](旧福島城下町地区氏神) → パセオ通り商店街 → 福島消防署 → 信夫山入山 → 黒沼神社(旧[[小山荒井村]]地区氏神)・[[福島縣護國神社]] → 信夫山ガイドセンター → [[羽黒神社]](頂上)
== イベント == 同日10日に『暁まいり福男福女競走』が催され、暁まいりで巡行する道筋に準ずるルートを競い合う。
== アクセス == 開催日当日は、臨時の無料駐車場が信夫山南峰の各所に設けられる
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.f-kankou.jp/cgi-bin/f-kankou/asobu/page.cgi?id=70 信夫三山暁まいり] - 福島市観光コンベンション協会 * [https://www.ntt-east.co.jp/fukushima/fbrari/031/05.html イベント「信夫三山暁まいり」] - NTT東日本福島支店
{{DEFAULTSORT:しのふやまさんさんあかつきまいり}} [[Category:東北地方開催の冬季イベント]] [[Category:福島県の祭り]] [[Category:福島市]]

== イベント == 同日10日に『暁まいり福男福女競走』が催され、暁まいりで巡行する道筋に準ずるルートを競い合う。
== アクセス == 開催日当日は、臨時の無料駐車場が信夫山南峰の各所に設けられる
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.f-kankou.jp/cgi-bin/f-kankou/asobu/page.cgi?id=70 信夫三山暁まいり] - 福島市観光コンベンション協会 * [https://www.ntt-east.co.jp/fukushima/fbrari/031/05.html イベント「信夫三山暁まいり」] - NTT東日本福島支店

revision.text:
{{otheruses|冬季2月の旧正月に行われる神事|夏季8月に催される祭事|福島わらじまつり}} '''信夫三山暁まいり'''（しのぶさんざんあかつきまいり）とは旧正月14日にあたる毎年[[2月10日]]から[[2月11日|11日]]に[[福島県]][[福島市]]中心部にある[[信夫山]]で行われる[[神事]]。 {{seealso|福島わらじまつり}} == 概要 == 福島市市街地に隣接する信夫山。その信夫山を構成する羽山、羽黒山、熊野山の三峰を祀る[[山岳信仰]]であり400年近くの歴史を有する祭事である。日本一の大わらじを奉納する独自性から福島県を代表する祭事のひとつとして知られている。担ぎ手は地元有志の御山敬神会、地元商工会議所が参加しており、子供用のわらじの担ぎ手に地元小学生が参加している。
主なルートは信夫山の北峰に位置する御山地区をスタートし、反時計回りに福島駅東口から福島市の中心市街地を経て、信夫山南峰から信夫山に入山。 頂上の羽黒神社まで奉納する。
メディアでの扱いとして主だったものは、毎年地元[[コミュニティ放送|コミュニティラジオ]]の『[[福島コミュニティ放送|FMポコ]]』で暁まいりの実況生放送を配信している（ネット配信による全国無料視聴可能）。
長さ12m、幅1,2ｍ、重さ約2tという巨大なわらじを運ぶ。
== 大わらじの巡行ルート == 御山作業所([[国道13号]]沿い) → [[佐藤工業 (福島市)|佐藤工業本社]]([[福島県道3号福島飯坂線|飯坂街道]]沿い) → [[福島成田山不動院]] → [[曽根田駅]]・[[曽根田ショッピングセンター|MAXふくしま]] → [[福島駅 (福島県)|福島駅]]東口 → 福島市金融街(レンガ通り) → [[福島稲荷神社]](旧福島城下町地区氏神) → パセオ通り商店街 → 福島消防署 → 信夫山入山 → 黒沼神社(旧[[小山荒井村]]地区氏神)・[[福島縣護國神社]] → 信夫山ガイドセンター → [[羽黒神社]](頂上)
== イベント == 同日10日に『暁まいり福男福女競走』が催され、暁まいりで巡行する道筋に準ずるルートを競い合う。
== アクセス == 開催日当日は、臨時の無料駐車場が信夫山南峰の各所に設けられる
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.f-kankou.jp/cgi-bin/f-kankou/asobu/page.cgi?id=70 信夫三山暁まいり] - 福島市観光コンベンション協会 * [https://www.ntt-east.co.jp/fukushima/fbrari/031/05.html イベント「信夫三山暁まいり」] - NTT東日本福島支店
{{DEFAULTSORT:しのふやまさんさんあかつきまいり}} [[Category:東北地方開催の冬季イベント]] [[Category:福島県の祭り]] [[Category:福島市の文化]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Shinobu Sanzan Akatsuki Mairi]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Shinobu Sanzan Akatsuki Mairi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:redirect]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Shinobu Sanzan Akatsuki Mairi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Shinobu Sanzan Akatsuki Mairi]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Shinobu Sanzan Akatsuki Mairi]][[Category:qq]][[ja:信夫三山暁まいり]]

{{otheruses|冬季2月の旧正月に行われる神事|夏季8月に催される祭事|福島わらじまつり}} '''信夫三山暁まいり'''（しのぶさんざんあかつきまいり）とは旧正月14日にあたる毎年[[2月10日]]から[[2月11日|11日]]に[[福島県]][[福島市]]中心部にある[[信夫山]]で行われる[[神事]]。 {{seealso|福島わらじまつり}} == 概要 == 福島市市街地に隣接する信夫山。その信夫山を構成する羽山、羽黒山、熊野山の三峰を祀る[[山岳信仰]]であり400年近くの歴史を有する祭事である。日本一の大わらじを奉納する独自性から福島県を代表する祭事のひとつとして知られている。担ぎ手は地元有志の御山敬神会、地元商工会議所が参加しており、子供用のわらじの担ぎ手に地元小学生が参加している。

長さ12m、幅1,2ｍ、重さ約2tという巨大なわらじを運ぶ。

== イベント == 同日10日に『暁まいり福男福女競走』が催され、暁まいりで巡行する道筋に準ずるルートを競い合う。
== アクセス == 開催日当日は、臨時の無料駐車場が信夫山南峰の各所に設けられる
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.f-kankou.jp/cgi-bin/f-kankou/asobu/page.cgi?id=70 信夫三山暁まいり] - 福島市観光コンベンション協会 * [https://www.ntt-east.co.jp/fukushima/fbrari/031/05.html イベント「信夫三山暁まいり」] - NTT東日本福島支店

{{otheruses|Shinobu Sanzan Akatsuki Mairi, a Shinto ritual held in February during the lunar New Year|Fukushima Waraji Festival, a summer event held in August|福島わらじまつり}}
'''Shinobu Sanzan Akatsuki Mairi''' (信夫三山暁まいり) is a {{ill|Shinto ritual|ja|神事}} held annually from {{ill|February 10|ja|2月10日}} to {{ill|February 11|ja|2月11日}}, coinciding with the 14th day of the lunar New Year. It takes place on {{ill|Mount Shinobu|ja|信夫山}}, located in the center of {{ill|Fukushima City|ja|福島市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
{{seealso|Fukushima Waraji Festival}}
== Overview == Mount Shinobu, adjacent to the urban area of Fukushima City, is venerated through {{ill|mountain worship|ja|山岳信仰}} that honors its three peaks: Hayama, Haguroyama, and Kumano-yama. This festival boasts nearly 400 years of history and is recognized as one of Fukushima Prefecture’s most representative events, distinguished by the offering of Japan’s largest straw sandal (*waraji*). The sandal bearers include local volunteers from the Oyama Keishinkai and members of the local chamber of commerce, with local elementary school students participating as bearers of the children’s *waraji*.
The main route starts in the Oyama district on the northern peak of Mount Shinobu, proceeds counterclockwise through the area near {{ill|Fukushima Station|ja|福島駅}} East Exit, traverses the city center, and enters Mount Shinobu from the southern peak, culminating with an offering at {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社}} at the summit.
In terms of media coverage, the event is broadcast live annually by the local {{ill|community radio|ja|コミュニティ放送}} station {{ill|FM Poco|ja|福島コミュニティ放送}}, available for free nationwide via online streaming.
A massive *waraji* measuring 12 meters in length, 1.2 meters in width, and weighing approximately 2 tons is carried during the event.
== Giant Waraji Procession Route == * Oyama Workshop (along {{ill|National Route 13 (Japan)|ja|国道13号}}) * {{ill|Sato Construction Headquarters|ja|佐藤工業 (福島市)}} (along {{ill|Fukushima Iizaka Line|ja|福島県道3号福島飯坂線}}) * {{ill|Fukushima Naritasan Fudoin|ja|福島成田山不動院}} * {{ill|Soneda Station|ja|曽根田駅}} / {{ill|MAX Fukushima|ja|曽根田ショッピングセンター}} * {{ill|Fukushima Station|ja|福島駅}} East Exit * Fukushima Financial District (Brick Street) * {{ill|Fukushima Inari Shrine|ja|福島稲荷神社}} (former guardian shrine of the Fukushima Castle town area) * Paseo Street Shopping District * Fukushima Fire Department * Entry to Mount Shinobu * {{ill|Kurōnuma Shrine|ja|黒沼神社}} (guardian shrine of the former {{ill|Koyama Arai Village|ja|小山荒井村}} area) * {{ill|Fukushima Gokoku Shrine|ja|福島縣護國神社}} * Mount Shinobu Guide Center * {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社}} (summit)
== Events == On the 10th, the "Akatsuki Mairi Fukuotoko Fukujyo Race" (*暁まいり福男福女競走*) is held, with participants competing along a route similar to the *waraji* procession.
== Access == On the day of the event, temporary free parking lots are set up at various locations around the southern peak of Mount Shinobu.
== External Links == * [Official Website of the Shinobu Sanzan Akatsuki Mairi (if available)]

* [http://www.f-kankou.jp/cgi-bin/f-kankou/asobu/page.cgi?id=70 信夫三山暁まいり] - 福島市観光コンベンション協会 * [https://www.ntt-east.co.jp/fukushima/fbrari/031/05.html イベント「信夫三山暁まいり」] - NTT東日本福島支店

{{otheruses|Shinobu Sanzan Akatsuki Mairi, a Shinto ritual held in February during the lunar New Year|Fukushima Waraji Festival, a summer event held in August|福島わらじまつり}}
'''Shinobu Sanzan Akatsuki Mairi''' (信夫三山暁まいり) is a {{ill|Shinto ritual|ja|神事}} held annually from {{ill|February 10|ja|2月10日}} to {{ill|February 11|ja|2月11日}}, coinciding with the 14th day of the lunar New Year. It takes place on {{ill|Mount Shinobu|ja|信夫山}}, located in the center of {{ill|Fukushima City|ja|福島市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
{{seealso|Fukushima Waraji Festival}}
== Overview == Mount Shinobu, adjacent to the urban area of Fukushima City, is venerated through {{ill|mountain worship|ja|山岳信仰}} that honors its three peaks: Hayama, Haguroyama, and Kumano-yama. This festival boasts nearly 400 years of history and is recognized as one of Fukushima Prefecture’s most representative events, distinguished by the offering of Japan’s largest straw sandal (*waraji*). The sandal bearers include local volunteers from the Oyama Keishinkai and members of the local chamber of commerce, with local elementary school students participating as bearers of the children’s *waraji*.
The main route starts in the Oyama district on the northern peak of Mount Shinobu, proceeds counterclockwise through the area near {{ill|Fukushima Station|ja|福島駅}} East Exit, traverses the city center, and enters Mount Shinobu from the southern peak, culminating with an offering at {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社}} at the summit.
In terms of media coverage, the event is broadcast live annually by the local {{ill|community radio|ja|コミュニティ放送}} station {{ill|FM Poco|ja|福島コミュニティ放送}}, available for free nationwide via online streaming.
A massive *waraji* measuring 12 meters in length, 1.2 meters in width, and weighing approximately 2 tons is carried during the event.
== Giant Waraji Procession Route == * Oyama Workshop (along {{ill|National Route 13 (Japan)|ja|国道13号}}) * {{ill|Sato Construction Headquarters|ja|佐藤工業 (福島市)}} (along {{ill|Fukushima Iizaka Line|ja|福島県道3号福島飯坂線}}) * {{ill|Fukushima Naritasan Fudoin|ja|福島成田山不動院}} * {{ill|Soneda Station|ja|曽根田駅}} / {{ill|MAX Fukushima|ja|曽根田ショッピングセンター}} * {{ill|Fukushima Station|ja|福島駅}} East Exit * Fukushima Financial District (Brick Street) * {{ill|Fukushima Inari Shrine|ja|福島稲荷神社}} (former guardian shrine of the Fukushima Castle town area) * Paseo Street Shopping District * Fukushima Fire Department * Entry to Mount Shinobu * {{ill|Kurōnuma Shrine|ja|黒沼神社}} (guardian shrine of the former {{ill|Koyama Arai Village|ja|小山荒井村}} area) * {{ill|Fukushima Gokoku Shrine|ja|福島縣護國神社}} * Mount Shinobu Guide Center * {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社}} (summit)
== Events == On the 10th, the "Akatsuki Mairi Fukuotoko Fukujyo Race" (*暁まいり福男福女競走*) is held, with participants competing along a route similar to the *waraji* procession.
== Access == On the day of the event, temporary free parking lots are set up at various locations around the southern peak of Mount Shinobu.
== External Links == * [Official Website of the Shinobu Sanzan Akatsuki Mairi (if available)]

* [http://www.f-kankou.jp/cgi-bin/f-kankou/asobu/page.cgi?id=70 信夫三山暁まいり] - 福島市観光コンベンション協会 * [https://www.ntt-east.co.jp/fukushima/fbrari/031/05.html イベント「信夫三山暁まいり」] - NTT東日本福島支店

== Events == On the 10th, the "Akatsuki Mairi Fukuotoko Fukujyo Race" (*暁まいり福男福女競走*) is held, with participants competing along a route similar to the *waraji* procession.

revision.text:
{{otheruses|Shinobu Sanzan Akatsuki Mairi, a Shinto ritual held in February during the lunar New Year|Fukushima Waraji Festival, a summer event held in August|福島わらじまつり}}
'''Shinobu Sanzan Akatsuki Mairi''' (信夫三山暁まいり) is a {{ill|Shinto ritual|ja|神事}} held annually from {{ill|February 10|ja|2月10日}} to {{ill|February 11|ja|2月11日}}, coinciding with the 14th day of the lunar New Year. It takes place on {{ill|Mount Shinobu|ja|信夫山}}, located in the center of {{ill|Fukushima City|ja|福島市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
{{seealso|Fukushima Waraji Festival}}
== Overview == Mount Shinobu, adjacent to the urban area of Fukushima City, is venerated through {{ill|mountain worship|ja|山岳信仰}} that honors its three peaks: Hayama, Haguroyama, and Kumano-yama. This festival boasts nearly 400 years of history and is recognized as one of Fukushima Prefecture’s most representative events, distinguished by the offering of Japan’s largest straw sandal (*waraji*). The sandal bearers include local volunteers from the Oyama Keishinkai and members of the local chamber of commerce, with local elementary school students participating as bearers of the children’s *waraji*.
The main route starts in the Oyama district on the northern peak of Mount Shinobu, proceeds counterclockwise through the area near {{ill|Fukushima Station|ja|福島駅}} East Exit, traverses the city center, and enters Mount Shinobu from the southern peak, culminating with an offering at {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社}} at the summit.
In terms of media coverage, the event is broadcast live annually by the local {{ill|community radio|ja|コミュニティ放送}} station {{ill|FM Poco|ja|福島コミュニティ放送}}, available for free nationwide via online streaming.
A massive *waraji* measuring 12 meters in length, 1.2 meters in width, and weighing approximately 2 tons is carried during the event.
== Giant Waraji Procession Route == * Oyama Workshop (along {{ill|National Route 13 (Japan)|ja|国道13号}}) * {{ill|Sato Construction Headquarters|ja|佐藤工業 (福島市)}} (along {{ill|Fukushima Iizaka Line|ja|福島県道3号福島飯坂線}}) * {{ill|Fukushima Naritasan Fudoin|ja|福島成田山不動院}} * {{ill|Soneda Station|ja|曽根田駅}} / {{ill|MAX Fukushima|ja|曽根田ショッピングセンター}} * {{ill|Fukushima Station|ja|福島駅}} East Exit * Fukushima Financial District (Brick Street) * {{ill|Fukushima Inari Shrine|ja|福島稲荷神社}} (former guardian shrine of the Fukushima Castle town area) * Paseo Street Shopping District * Fukushima Fire Department * Entry to Mount Shinobu * {{ill|Kurōnuma Shrine|ja|黒沼神社}} (guardian shrine of the former {{ill|Koyama Arai Village|ja|小山荒井村}} area) * {{ill|Fukushima Gokoku Shrine|ja|福島縣護國神社}} * Mount Shinobu Guide Center * {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社}} (summit)
== Events == On the 10th, the "Akatsuki Mairi Fukuotoko Fukujyo Race" (*暁まいり福男福女競走*) is held, with participants competing along a route similar to the *waraji* procession.
== Access == On the day of the event, temporary free parking lots are set up at various locations around the southern peak of Mount Shinobu.
== External Links == * [Official Website of the Shinobu Sanzan Akatsuki Mairi (if available)]

{{otheruses|Shinobu Sanzan Akatsuki Mairi, a Shinto ritual held in February during the lunar New Year|Fukushima Waraji Festival, a summer event held in August|福島わらじまつり}}
'''Shinobu Sanzan Akatsuki Mairi''' (信夫三山暁まいり) is a {{ill|Shinto ritual|ja|神事}} held annually from {{ill|February 10|ja|2月10日}} to {{ill|February 11|ja|2月11日}}, coinciding with the 14th day of the lunar New Year. It takes place on {{ill|Mount Shinobu|ja|信夫山}}, located in the center of {{ill|Fukushima City|ja|福島市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
{{seealso|Fukushima Waraji Festival}}
== Overview == Mount Shinobu, adjacent to the urban area of Fukushima City, is venerated through {{ill|mountain worship|ja|山岳信仰}} that honors its three peaks: Hayama, Haguroyama, and Kumano-yama. This festival boasts nearly 400 years of history and is recognized as one of Fukushima Prefecture’s most representative events, distinguished by the offering of Japan’s largest straw sandal (*waraji*). The sandal bearers include local volunteers from the Oyama Keishinkai and members of the local chamber of commerce, with local elementary school students participating as bearers of the children’s *waraji*.
The main route starts in the Oyama district on the northern peak of Mount Shinobu, proceeds counterclockwise through the area near {{ill|Fukushima Station|ja|福島駅}} East Exit, traverses the city center, and enters Mount Shinobu from the southern peak, culminating with an offering at {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社}} at the summit.
In terms of media coverage, the event is broadcast live annually by the local {{ill|community radio|ja|コミュニティ放送}} station {{ill|FM Poco|ja|福島コミュニティ放送}}, available for free nationwide via online streaming.
A massive *waraji* measuring 12 meters in length, 1.2 meters in width, and weighing approximately 2 tons is carried during the event.
== Giant Waraji Procession Route == * Oyama Workshop (along {{ill|National Route 13 (Japan)|ja|国道13号}}) * {{ill|Sato Construction Headquarters|ja|佐藤工業 (福島市)}} (along {{ill|Fukushima Iizaka Line|ja|福島県道3号福島飯坂線}}) * {{ill|Fukushima Naritasan Fudoin|ja|福島成田山不動院}} * {{ill|Soneda Station|ja|曽根田駅}} / {{ill|MAX Fukushima|ja|曽根田ショッピングセンター}} * {{ill|Fukushima Station|ja|福島駅}} East Exit * Fukushima Financial District (Brick Street) * {{ill|Fukushima Inari Shrine|ja|福島稲荷神社}} (former guardian shrine of the Fukushima Castle town area) * Paseo Street Shopping District * Fukushima Fire Department * Entry to Mount Shinobu * {{ill|Kurōnuma Shrine|ja|黒沼神社}} (guardian shrine of the former {{ill|Koyama Arai Village|ja|小山荒井村}} area) * {{ill|Fukushima Gokoku Shrine|ja|福島縣護國神社}} * Mount Shinobu Guide Center * {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社}} (summit)
== Events == On the 10th, the "Akatsuki Mairi Fukuotoko Fukujyo Race" (*暁まいり福男福女競走*) is held, with participants competing along a route similar to the *waraji* procession.
== Access == On the day of the event, temporary free parking lots are set up at various locations around the southern peak of Mount Shinobu.
== External Links == * [Official Website of the Shinobu Sanzan Akatsuki Mairi (if available)]

{{otheruses|Shinobu Sanzan Akatsuki Mairi, a Shinto ritual held in February during the lunar New Year|Fukushima Waraji Festival, a summer event held in August|福島わらじまつり}}
'''Shinobu Sanzan Akatsuki Mairi''' (信夫三山暁まいり) is a {{ill|Shinto ritual|ja|神事}} held annually from {{ill|February 10|ja|2月10日}} to {{ill|February 11|ja|2月11日}}, coinciding with the 14th day of the lunar New Year. It takes place on {{ill|Mount Shinobu|ja|信夫山}}, located in the center of {{ill|Fukushima City|ja|福島市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
{{seealso|Fukushima Waraji Festival}}
== Overview == Mount Shinobu, adjacent to the urban area of Fukushima City, is venerated through {{ill|mountain worship|ja|山岳信仰}} that honors its three peaks: Hayama, Haguroyama, and Kumano-yama. This festival boasts nearly 400 years of history and is recognized as one of Fukushima Prefecture’s most representative events, distinguished by the offering of Japan’s largest straw sandal (*waraji*). The sandal bearers include local volunteers from the Oyama Keishinkai and members of the local chamber of commerce, with local elementary school students participating as bearers of the children’s *waraji*.
The main route starts in the Oyama district on the northern peak of Mount Shinobu, proceeds counterclockwise through the area near {{ill|Fukushima Station|ja|福島駅}} East Exit, traverses the city center, and enters Mount Shinobu from the southern peak, culminating with an offering at {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社}} at the summit.
In terms of media coverage, the event is broadcast live annually by the local {{ill|community radio|ja|コミュニティ放送}} station {{ill|FM Poco|ja|福島コミュニティ放送}}, available for free nationwide via online streaming.
A massive *waraji* measuring 12 meters in length, 1.2 meters in width, and weighing approximately 2 tons is carried during the event.
== Giant Waraji Procession Route == * Oyama Workshop (along {{ill|National Route 13 (Japan)|ja|国道13号}}) * {{ill|Sato Construction Headquarters|ja|佐藤工業 (福島市)}} (along {{ill|Fukushima Iizaka Line|ja|福島県道3号福島飯坂線}}) * {{ill|Fukushima Naritasan Fudoin|ja|福島成田山不動院}} * {{ill|Soneda Station|ja|曽根田駅}} / {{ill|MAX Fukushima|ja|曽根田ショッピングセンター}} * {{ill|Fukushima Station|ja|福島駅}} East Exit * Fukushima Financial District (Brick Street) * {{ill|Fukushima Inari Shrine|ja|福島稲荷神社}} (former guardian shrine of the Fukushima Castle town area) * Paseo Street Shopping District * Fukushima Fire Department * Entry to Mount Shinobu * {{ill|Kurōnuma Shrine|ja|黒沼神社}} (guardian shrine of the former {{ill|Koyama Arai Village|ja|小山荒井村}} area) * {{ill|Fukushima Gokoku Shrine|ja|福島縣護國神社}} * Mount Shinobu Guide Center * {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社}} (summit)
== Events == On the 10th, the "Akatsuki Mairi Fukuotoko Fukujyo Race" (*暁まいり福男福女競走*) is held, with participants competing along a route similar to the *waraji* procession.
== Access == On the day of the event, temporary free parking lots are set up at various locations around the southern peak of Mount Shinobu.
== External Links == * [Official Website of the Shinobu Sanzan Akatsuki Mairi (if available)]

It takes place on {{ill|Mount Shinobu|ja|信夫山}}, located in the center of {{ill|Fukushima City|ja|福島市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.

{{otheruses|Shinobu Sanzan Akatsuki Mairi, a Shinto ritual held in February during the lunar New Year|Fukushima Waraji Festival, a summer event held in August|福島わらじまつり}}
'''Shinobu Sanzan Akatsuki Mairi''' (信夫三山暁まいり) is a {{ill|Shinto ritual|ja|神事}} held annually from {{ill|February 10|ja|2月10日}} to {{ill|February 11|ja|2月11日}}, coinciding with the 14th day of the lunar New Year. It takes place on {{ill|Mount Shinobu|ja|信夫山}}, located in the center of {{ill|Fukushima City|ja|福島市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
{{seealso|Fukushima Waraji Festival}}
== Overview == Mount Shinobu, adjacent to the urban area of Fukushima City, is venerated through {{ill|mountain worship|ja|山岳信仰}} that honors its three peaks: Hayama, Haguroyama, and Kumano-yama. This festival boasts nearly 400 years of history and is recognized as one of Fukushima Prefecture’s most representative events, distinguished by the offering of Japan’s largest straw sandal (*waraji*). The sandal bearers include local volunteers from the Oyama Keishinkai and members of the local chamber of commerce, with local elementary school students participating as bearers of the children’s *waraji*.
The main route starts in the Oyama district on the northern peak of Mount Shinobu, proceeds counterclockwise through the area near {{ill|Fukushima Station|ja|福島駅}} East Exit, traverses the city center, and enters Mount Shinobu from the southern peak, culminating with an offering at {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社}} at the summit.
In terms of media coverage, the event is broadcast live annually by the local {{ill|community radio|ja|コミュニティ放送}} station {{ill|FM Poco|ja|福島コミュニティ放送}}, available for free nationwide via online streaming.
A massive *waraji* measuring 12 meters in length, 1.2 meters in width, and weighing approximately 2 tons is carried during the event.
== Giant Waraji Procession Route == * Oyama Workshop (along {{ill|National Route 13 (Japan)|ja|国道13号}}) * {{ill|Sato Construction Headquarters|ja|佐藤工業 (福島市)}} (along {{ill|Fukushima Iizaka Line|ja|福島県道3号福島飯坂線}}) * {{ill|Fukushima Naritasan Fudoin|ja|福島成田山不動院}} * {{ill|Soneda Station|ja|曽根田駅}} / {{ill|MAX Fukushima|ja|曽根田ショッピングセンター}} * {{ill|Fukushima Station|ja|福島駅}} East Exit * Fukushima Financial District (Brick Street) * {{ill|Fukushima Inari Shrine|ja|福島稲荷神社}} (former guardian shrine of the Fukushima Castle town area) * Paseo Street Shopping District * Fukushima Fire Department * Entry to Mount Shinobu * {{ill|Kurōnuma Shrine|ja|黒沼神社}} (guardian shrine of the former {{ill|Koyama Arai Village|ja|小山荒井村}} area) * {{ill|Fukushima Gokoku Shrine|ja|福島縣護國神社}} * Mount Shinobu Guide Center * {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社}} (summit)
== Events == On the 10th, the "Akatsuki Mairi Fukuotoko Fukujyo Race" (*暁まいり福男福女競走*) is held, with participants competing along a route similar to the *waraji* procession.
== Access == On the day of the event, temporary free parking lots are set up at various locations around the southern peak of Mount Shinobu.
== External Links == * [Official Website of the Shinobu Sanzan Akatsuki Mairi (if available)]

== Overview == Mount Shinobu, adjacent to the urban area of Fukushima City, is venerated through {{ill|mountain worship|ja|山岳信仰}} that honors its three peaks: Hayama, Haguroyama, and Kumano-yama. This festival boasts nearly 400 years of history and is recognized as one of Fukushima Prefecture’s most representative events, distinguished by the offering of Japan’s largest straw sandal (*waraji*). The sandal bearers include local volunteers from the Oyama Keishinkai and members of the local chamber of commerce, with local elementary school students participating as bearers of the children’s *waraji*.
The main route starts in the Oyama district on the northern peak of Mount Shinobu, proceeds counterclockwise through the area near {{ill|Fukushima Station|ja|福島駅}} East Exit, traverses the city center, and enters Mount Shinobu from the southern peak, culminating with an offering at {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社}} at the summit.
In terms of media coverage, the event is broadcast live annually by the local {{ill|community radio|ja|コミュニティ放送}} station {{ill|FM Poco|ja|福島コミュニティ放送}}, available for free nationwide via online streaming.
A massive *waraji* measuring 12 meters in length, 1.2 meters in width, and weighing approximately 2 tons is carried during the event.
== Giant Waraji Procession Route == * Oyama Workshop (along {{ill|National Route 13 (Japan)|ja|国道13号}}) * {{ill|Sato Construction Headquarters|ja|佐藤工業 (福島市)}} (along {{ill|Fukushima Iizaka Line|ja|福島県道3号福島飯坂線}}) * {{ill|Fukushima Naritasan Fudoin|ja|福島成田山不動院}} * {{ill|Soneda Station|ja|曽根田駅}} / {{ill|MAX Fukushima|ja|曽根田ショッピングセンター}} * {{ill|Fukushima Station|ja|福島駅}} East Exit * Fukushima Financial District (Brick Street) * {{ill|Fukushima Inari Shrine|ja|福島稲荷神社}} (former guardian shrine of the Fukushima Castle town area) * Paseo Street Shopping District * Fukushima Fire Department * Entry to Mount Shinobu * {{ill|Kurōnuma Shrine|ja|黒沼神社}} (guardian shrine of the former {{ill|Koyama Arai Village|ja|小山荒井村}} area) * {{ill|Fukushima Gokoku Shrine|ja|福島縣護國神社}} * Mount Shinobu Guide Center * {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社}} (summit)

{{otheruses|Shinobu Sanzan Akatsuki Mairi, a Shinto ritual held in February during the lunar New Year|Fukushima Waraji Festival, a summer event held in August|福島わらじまつり}}
'''Shinobu Sanzan Akatsuki Mairi''' (信夫三山暁まいり) is a {{ill|Shinto ritual|ja|神事}} held annually from {{ill|February 10|ja|2月10日}} to {{ill|February 11|ja|2月11日}}, coinciding with the 14th day of the lunar New Year. It takes place on {{ill|Mount Shinobu|ja|信夫山}}, located in the center of {{ill|Fukushima City|ja|福島市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
{{seealso|Fukushima Waraji Festival}}
== Overview == Mount Shinobu, adjacent to the urban area of Fukushima City, is venerated through {{ill|mountain worship|ja|山岳信仰}} that honors its three peaks: Hayama, Haguroyama, and Kumano-yama. This festival boasts nearly 400 years of history and is recognized as one of Fukushima Prefecture’s most representative events, distinguished by the offering of Japan’s largest straw sandal (*waraji*). The sandal bearers include local volunteers from the Oyama Keishinkai and members of the local chamber of commerce, with local elementary school students participating as bearers of the children’s *waraji*.
The main route starts in the Oyama district on the northern peak of Mount Shinobu, proceeds counterclockwise through the area near {{ill|Fukushima Station|ja|福島駅}} East Exit, traverses the city center, and enters Mount Shinobu from the southern peak, culminating with an offering at {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社}} at the summit.
In terms of media coverage, the event is broadcast live annually by the local {{ill|community radio|ja|コミュニティ放送}} station {{ill|FM Poco|ja|福島コミュニティ放送}}, available for free nationwide via online streaming.
A massive *waraji* measuring 12 meters in length, 1.2 meters in width, and weighing approximately 2 tons is carried during the event.
== Giant Waraji Procession Route == * Oyama Workshop (along {{ill|National Route 13 (Japan)|ja|国道13号}}) * {{ill|Sato Construction Headquarters|ja|佐藤工業 (福島市)}} (along {{ill|Fukushima Iizaka Line|ja|福島県道3号福島飯坂線}}) * {{ill|Fukushima Naritasan Fudoin|ja|福島成田山不動院}} * {{ill|Soneda Station|ja|曽根田駅}} / {{ill|MAX Fukushima|ja|曽根田ショッピングセンター}} * {{ill|Fukushima Station|ja|福島駅}} East Exit * Fukushima Financial District (Brick Street) * {{ill|Fukushima Inari Shrine|ja|福島稲荷神社}} (former guardian shrine of the Fukushima Castle town area) * Paseo Street Shopping District * Fukushima Fire Department * Entry to Mount Shinobu * {{ill|Kurōnuma Shrine|ja|黒沼神社}} (guardian shrine of the former {{ill|Koyama Arai Village|ja|小山荒井村}} area) * {{ill|Fukushima Gokoku Shrine|ja|福島縣護國神社}} * Mount Shinobu Guide Center * {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社}} (summit)
== Events == On the 10th, the "Akatsuki Mairi Fukuotoko Fukujyo Race" (*暁まいり福男福女競走*) is held, with participants competing along a route similar to the *waraji* procession.
== Access == On the day of the event, temporary free parking lots are set up at various locations around the southern peak of Mount Shinobu.
== External Links == * [Official Website of the Shinobu Sanzan Akatsuki Mairi (if available)]

== Overview == Mount Shinobu, adjacent to the urban area of Fukushima City, is venerated through {{ill|mountain worship|ja|山岳信仰|12=draft}} that honors its three peaks: Hayama, Haguroyama, and Kumano-yama. This festival boasts nearly 400 years of history and is recognized as one of Fukushima Prefecture’s most representative events, distinguished by the offering of Japan’s largest straw sandal (*waraji*). The sandal bearers include local volunteers from the Oyama Keishinkai and members of the local chamber of commerce, with local elementary school students participating as bearers of the children’s *waraji*.
The main route starts in the Oyama district on the northern peak of Mount Shinobu, proceeds counterclockwise through the area near {{ill|Fukushima Station|ja|福島駅|12=draft}} East Exit, traverses the city center, and enters Mount Shinobu from the southern peak, culminating with an offering at {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|12=draft}} at the summit.
In terms of media coverage, the event is broadcast live annually by the local {{ill|community radio|ja|コミュニティ放送|12=draft}} station {{ill|FM Poco|ja|福島コミュニティ放送|12=draft}}, available for free nationwide via online streaming.
A massive *waraji* measuring 12 meters in length, 1.2 meters in width, and weighing approximately 2 tons is carried during the event.
== Giant Waraji Procession Route == * Oyama Workshop (along {{ill|National Route 13 (Japan)|ja|国道13号|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Sato Construction Headquarters|ja|佐藤工業 (福島市)|12=draft}} (along {{ill|Fukushima Iizaka Line|ja|福島県道3号福島飯坂線|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Fukushima Naritasan Fudoin|ja|福島成田山不動院|12=draft}} * {{ill|Soneda Station|ja|曽根田駅|12=draft}} / {{ill|MAX Fukushima|ja|曽根田ショッピングセンター|12=draft}} * {{ill|Fukushima Station|ja|福島駅|12=draft}} East Exit * Fukushima Financial District (Brick Street) * {{ill|Fukushima Inari Shrine|ja|福島稲荷神社|12=draft}} (former guardian shrine of the Fukushima Castle town area) * Paseo Street Shopping District * Fukushima Fire Department * Entry to Mount Shinobu * {{ill|Kurōnuma Shrine|ja|黒沼神社|12=draft}} (guardian shrine of the former {{ill|Koyama Arai Village|ja|小山荒井村|12=draft}} area) * {{ill|Fukushima Gokoku Shrine|ja|福島縣護國神社|12=draft}} * Mount Shinobu Guide Center * {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|12=draft}} (summit)
== Events == On the 10th, the "Akatsuki Mairi Fukuotoko Fukujyo Race" (*暁まいり福男福女競走*) is held, with participants competing along a route similar to the *waraji* procession.
== Access == On the day of the event, temporary free parking lots are set up at various locations around the southern peak of Mount Shinobu.
== External Links == * [Official Website of the Shinobu Sanzan Akatsuki Mairi (if available)]
* [http://www.f-kankou.jp/cgi-bin/f-kankou/asobu/page.cgi?id=70 信夫三山暁まいり] - 福島市観光コンベンション協会 * [https://www.ntt-east.co.jp/fukushima/fbrari/031/05.html イベント「信夫三山暁まいり」] - NTT東日本福島支店 <!--Shinobu Sanzan Akatsuki Mairi-->

'''Shinobu Sanzan Akatsuki Mairi''' (信夫三山暁まいり) is a {{ill|Shinto ritual|ja|神事|12=draft}} held annually from {{ill|February 10|ja|2月10日|12=draft}} to {{ill|February 11|ja|2月11日|12=draft}}, coinciding with the 14th day of the lunar New Year. It takes place on {{ill|Mount Shinobu|ja|信夫山|12=draft}}, located in the center of {{ill|Fukushima City|ja|福島市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|12=draft}}.

== Overview == Mount Shinobu, adjacent to the urban area of Fukushima City, is venerated through {{ill|mountain worship|ja|山岳信仰|12=draft}} that honors its three peaks: Hayama, Haguroyama, and Kumano-yama. This festival boasts nearly 400 years of history and is recognized as one of Fukushima Prefecture’s most representative events, distinguished by the offering of Japan’s largest straw sandal (*waraji*). The sandal bearers include local volunteers from the Oyama Keishinkai and members of the local chamber of commerce, with local elementary school students participating as bearers of the children’s *waraji*.
The main route starts in the Oyama district on the northern peak of Mount Shinobu, proceeds counterclockwise through the area near {{ill|Fukushima Station|ja|福島駅|12=draft}} East Exit, traverses the city center, and enters Mount Shinobu from the southern peak, culminating with an offering at {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|12=draft}} at the summit.
In terms of media coverage, the event is broadcast live annually by the local {{ill|community radio|ja|コミュニティ放送|12=draft}} station {{ill|FM Poco|ja|福島コミュニティ放送|12=draft}}, available for free nationwide via online streaming.
A massive *waraji* measuring 12 meters in length, 1.2 meters in width, and weighing approximately 2 tons is carried during the event.

== Events == On the 10th, the "Akatsuki Mairi Fukuotoko Fukujyo Race" (*暁まいり福男福女競走*) is held, with participants competing along a route similar to the *waraji* procession.
== Access == On the day of the event, temporary free parking lots are set up at various locations around the southern peak of Mount Shinobu.
== External Links == * [Official Website of the Shinobu Sanzan Akatsuki Mairi (if available)]
* [http://www.f-kankou.jp/cgi-bin/f-kankou/asobu/page.cgi?id=70 信夫三山暁まいり] - 福島市観光コンベンション協会 * [https://www.ntt-east.co.jp/fukushima/fbrari/031/05.html イベント「信夫三山暁まいり」] - NTT東日本福島支店 <!--Shinobu Sanzan Akatsuki Mairi-->

revision.text:
{{otheruses|冬季2月の旧正月に行われる神事|夏季8月に催される祭事|福島わらじまつり}} '''信夫三山暁まいり'''（しのぶさんざんあかつきまいり）とは旧正月14日にあたる毎年[[2月10日]]から[[2月11日|11日]]に[[福島県]][[福島市]]中心部にある[[信夫山]]で行われる[[神事]]。 {{seealso|福島わらじまつり}} == 概要 == 福島市市街地に隣接する信夫山。その信夫山を構成する羽山、羽黒山、熊野山の三峰を祀る[[山岳信仰]]であり400年近くの歴史を有する祭事である。日本一の大わらじを奉納する独自性から福島県を代表する祭事のひとつとして知られている。担ぎ手は地元有志の御山敬神会、地元商工会議所が参加しており、子供用のわらじの担ぎ手に地元小学生が参加している。
主なルートは信夫山の北峰に位置する御山地区をスタートし、反時計回りに福島駅東口から福島市の中心市街地を経て、信夫山南峰から信夫山に入山。 頂上の羽黒神社まで奉納する。
メディアでの扱いとして主だったものは、毎年地元[[コミュニティ放送|コミュニティラジオ]]の『[[福島コミュニティ放送|FMポコ]]』で暁まいりの実況生放送を配信している（ネット配信による全国無料視聴可能）。
長さ12m、幅1,2ｍ、重さ約2tという巨大なわらじを運ぶ。
== 大わらじの巡行ルート == 御山作業所([[国道13号]]沿い) → [[佐藤工業 (福島市)|佐藤工業本社]]([[福島県道3号福島飯坂線|飯坂街道]]沿い) → [[福島成田山不動院]] → [[曽根田駅]]・[[曽根田ショッピングセンター|MAXふくしま]] → [[福島駅 (福島県)|福島駅]]東口 → 福島市金融街(レンガ通り) → [[福島稲荷神社]](旧福島城下町地区氏神) → パセオ通り商店街 → 福島消防署 → 信夫山入山 → 黒沼神社(旧[[小山荒井村]]地区氏神)・[[福島縣護國神社]] → 信夫山ガイドセンター → [[羽黒神社]](頂上)
== イベント == 同日10日に『暁まいり福男福女競走』が催され、暁まいりで巡行する道筋に準ずるルートを競い合う。
== アクセス == 開催日当日は、臨時の無料駐車場が信夫山南峰の各所に設けられる
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.f-kankou.jp/cgi-bin/f-kankou/asobu/page.cgi?id=70 信夫三山暁まいり] - 福島市観光コンベンション協会 * [https://www.ntt-east.co.jp/fukushima/fbrari/031/05.html イベント「信夫三山暁まいり」] - NTT東日本福島支店
{{DEFAULTSORT:しのふやまさんさんあかつきまいり}} [[Category:東北地方開催の冬季イベント]] [[Category:福島県の祭り]] [[Category:福島市の文化]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[信夫三山暁まいり]] to [[Shinobu Sanzan Akatsuki Mairi]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import

{{DEFAULTSORT:しのふやまさんさんあかつきまいり}} [[Category:東北地方開催の冬季イベント]] [[Category:福島県の祭り]] [[Category:福島市の文化]]

== Giant Waraji Procession Route == * Oyama Workshop (along {{ill|National Route 13 (Japan)|ja|国道13号|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Sato Construction Headquarters|ja|佐藤工業 (福島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (along {{ill|Fukushima Iizaka Line|ja|福島県道3号福島飯坂線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Fukushima Naritasan Fudoin|ja|福島成田山不動院|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Soneda Station|ja|曽根田駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} / {{ill|MAX Fukushima|ja|曽根田ショッピングセンター|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Fukushima Station|ja|福島駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} East Exit * Fukushima Financial District (Brick Street) * {{ill|Fukushima Inari Shrine|ja|福島稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (former guardian shrine of the Fukushima Castle town area) * Paseo Street Shopping District * Fukushima Fire Department * Entry to Mount Shinobu * {{ill|Kurōnuma Shrine|ja|黒沼神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (guardian shrine of the former {{ill|Koyama Arai Village|ja|小山荒井村|qq=draft|qq=draft}} area) * {{ill|Fukushima Gokoku Shrine|ja|福島縣護國神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Mount Shinobu Guide Center * {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (summit)
== Events == On the 10th, the "Akatsuki Mairi Fukuotoko Fukujyo Race" (*暁まいり福男福女競走*) is held, with participants competing along a route similar to the *waraji* procession.
== Access == On the day of the event, temporary free parking lots are set up at various locations around the southern peak of Mount Shinobu.

{{otheruses|Shinobu Sanzan Akatsuki Mairi, a Shinto ritual held in February during the lunar New Year|Fukushima Waraji Festival, a summer event held in August|福島わらじまつり}}
'''Shinobu Sanzan Akatsuki Mairi''' (信夫三山暁まいり) is a {{ill|Shinto ritual|ja|神事|qq=draft|qq=draft}} held annually from {{ill|February 10|ja|2月10日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to {{ill|February 11|ja|2月11日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, coinciding with the 14th day of the lunar New Year. It takes place on {{ill|Mount Shinobu|ja|信夫山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, located in the center of {{ill|Fukushima City|ja|福島市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
{{seealso|Fukushima Waraji Festival}}
== Overview == Mount Shinobu, adjacent to the urban area of Fukushima City, is venerated through {{ill|mountain worship|ja|山岳信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft}} that honors its three peaks: Hayama, Haguroyama, and Kumano-yama. This festival boasts nearly 400 years of history and is recognized as one of Fukushima Prefecture’s most representative events, distinguished by the offering of Japan’s largest straw sandal (*waraji*). The sandal bearers include local volunteers from the Oyama Keishinkai and members of the local chamber of commerce, with local elementary school students participating as bearers of the children’s *waraji*.
The main route starts in the Oyama district on the northern peak of Mount Shinobu, proceeds counterclockwise through the area near {{ill|Fukushima Station|ja|福島駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} East Exit, traverses the city center, and enters Mount Shinobu from the southern peak, culminating with an offering at {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at the summit.
In terms of media coverage, the event is broadcast live annually by the local {{ill|community radio|ja|コミュニティ放送|qq=draft|qq=draft}} station {{ill|FM Poco|ja|福島コミュニティ放送|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, available for free nationwide via online streaming.
A massive *waraji* measuring 12 meters in length, 1.2 meters in width, and weighing approximately 2 tons is carried during the event.
== Giant Waraji Procession Route == * Oyama Workshop (along {{ill|National Route 13 (Japan)|ja|国道13号|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Sato Construction Headquarters|ja|佐藤工業 (福島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (along {{ill|Fukushima Iizaka Line|ja|福島県道3号福島飯坂線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Fukushima Naritasan Fudoin|ja|福島成田山不動院|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Soneda Station|ja|曽根田駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} / {{ill|MAX Fukushima|ja|曽根田ショッピングセンター|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Fukushima Station|ja|福島駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} East Exit * Fukushima Financial District (Brick Street) * {{ill|Fukushima Inari Shrine|ja|福島稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (former guardian shrine of the Fukushima Castle town area) * Paseo Street Shopping District * Fukushima Fire Department * Entry to Mount Shinobu * {{ill|Kurōnuma Shrine|ja|黒沼神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (guardian shrine of the former {{ill|Koyama Arai Village|ja|小山荒井村|qq=draft|qq=draft}} area) * {{ill|Fukushima Gokoku Shrine|ja|福島縣護國神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Mount Shinobu Guide Center * {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (summit)
== Events == On the 10th, the "Akatsuki Mairi Fukuotoko Fukujyo Race" (*暁まいり福男福女競走*) is held, with participants competing along a route similar to the *waraji* procession.
== Access == On the day of the event, temporary free parking lots are set up at various locations around the southern peak of Mount Shinobu.

* [http://www.f-kankou.jp/cgi-bin/f-kankou/asobu/page.cgi?id=70 信夫三山暁まいり] - 福島市観光コンベンション協会 * [https://www.ntt-east.co.jp/fukushima/fbrari/031/05.html イベント「信夫三山暁まいり」] - NTT東日本福島支店

The main route starts in the Oyama district on the northern peak of Mount Shinobu, proceeds counterclockwise through the area near {{ill|Fukushima Station|ja|福島駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} East Exit, traverses the city center, and enters Mount Shinobu from the southern peak, culminating with an offering at {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at the summit.
In terms of media coverage, the event is broadcast live annually by the local {{ill|community radio|ja|コミュニティ放送|qq=draft|qq=draft}} station {{ill|FM Poco|ja|福島コミュニティ放送|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, available for free nationwide via online streaming.
A massive *waraji* measuring 12 meters in length, 1.2 meters in width, and weighing approximately 2 tons is carried during the event.
== Giant Waraji Procession Route == * Oyama Workshop (along {{ill|National Route 13 (Japan)|ja|国道13号|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Sato Construction Headquarters|ja|佐藤工業 (福島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (along {{ill|Fukushima Iizaka Line|ja|福島県道3号福島飯坂線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Fukushima Naritasan Fudoin|ja|福島成田山不動院|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Soneda Station|ja|曽根田駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} / {{ill|MAX Fukushima|ja|曽根田ショッピングセンター|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Fukushima Station|ja|福島駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} East Exit * Fukushima Financial District (Brick Street) * {{ill|Fukushima Inari Shrine|ja|福島稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (former guardian shrine of the Fukushima Castle town area) * Paseo Street Shopping District * Fukushima Fire Department * Entry to Mount Shinobu * {{ill|Kurōnuma Shrine|ja|黒沼神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (guardian shrine of the former {{ill|Koyama Arai Village|ja|小山荒井村|qq=draft|qq=draft}} area) * {{ill|Fukushima Gokoku Shrine|ja|福島縣護國神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Mount Shinobu Guide Center * {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (summit)
== Events == On the 10th, the "Akatsuki Mairi Fukuotoko Fukujyo Race" (*暁まいり福男福女競走*) is held, with participants competing along a route similar to the *waraji* procession.
== Access == On the day of the event, temporary free parking lots are set up at various locations around the southern peak of Mount Shinobu.
== External Links == * [Official Website of the Shinobu Sanzan Akatsuki Mairi (if available)]
* [http://www.f-kankou.jp/cgi-bin/f-kankou/asobu/page.cgi?id=70 信夫三山暁まいり] - 福島市観光コンベンション協会 * [https://www.ntt-east.co.jp/fukushima/fbrari/031/05.html イベント「信夫三山暁まいり」] - NTT東日本福島支店

{{otheruses|Shinobu Sanzan Akatsuki Mairi, a Shinto ritual held in February during the lunar New Year|Fukushima Waraji Festival, a summer event held in August|福島わらじまつり}}
'''Shinobu Sanzan Akatsuki Mairi''' (信夫三山暁まいり) is a {{ill|Shinto ritual|ja|神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto ritual|qq=}} held annually from [[:en:February 10|February 10]] to [[:en:February 11|February 11]], coinciding with the 14th day of the lunar New Year. It takes place on [[:en:Mount Shinobu|Mount Shinobu]], located in the center of [[:en:Fukushima (city)|Fukushima City]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]].
{{seealso|Fukushima Waraji Festival}}
== Overview == Mount Shinobu, adjacent to the urban area of Fukushima City, is venerated through [[:en:Mountain worship|mountain worship]] that honors its three peaks: Hayama, Haguroyama, and Kumano-yama. This festival boasts nearly 400 years of history and is recognized as one of Fukushima Prefecture’s most representative events, distinguished by the offering of Japan’s largest straw sandal (*waraji*). The sandal bearers include local volunteers from the Oyama Keishinkai and members of the local chamber of commerce, with local elementary school students participating as bearers of the children’s *waraji*.
The main route starts in the Oyama district on the northern peak of Mount Shinobu, proceeds counterclockwise through the area near [[:en:Fukushima Station|Fukushima Station]] East Exit, traverses the city center, and enters Mount Shinobu from the southern peak, culminating with an offering at {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine|qq=}} at the summit.
In terms of media coverage, the event is broadcast live annually by the local [[:en:Community radio|community radio]] station {{ill|FM Poco|ja|福島コミュニティ放送|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/FM Poco|qq=}}, available for free nationwide via online streaming.
A massive *waraji* measuring 12 meters in length, 1.2 meters in width, and weighing approximately 2 tons is carried during the event.

== Access == On the day of the event, temporary free parking lots are set up at various locations around the southern peak of Mount Shinobu.

* [http://www.f-kankou.jp/cgi-bin/f-kankou/asobu/page.cgi?id=70 信夫三山暁まいり] - 福島市観光コンベンション協会 * [https://www.ntt-east.co.jp/fukushima/fbrari/031/05.html イベント「信夫三山暁まいり」] - NTT東日本福島支店

revision.text:
{{otheruses|Shinobu Sanzan Akatsuki Mairi, a Shinto ritual held in February during the lunar New Year|Fukushima Waraji Festival, a summer event held in August|福島わらじまつり}}
'''Shinobu Sanzan Akatsuki Mairi''' (信夫三山暁まいり) is a {{ill|Shinto ritual|ja|神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto ritual|qq=}} held annually from [[:en:February 10|February 10]] to [[:en:February 11|February 11]], coinciding with the 14th day of the lunar New Year. It takes place on [[:en:Mount Shinobu|Mount Shinobu]], located in the center of [[:en:Fukushima (city)|Fukushima City]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]].
{{seealso|Fukushima Waraji Festival}}
== Overview == Mount Shinobu, adjacent to the urban area of Fukushima City, is venerated through [[:en:Mountain worship|mountain worship]] that honors its three peaks: Hayama, Haguroyama, and Kumano-yama. This festival boasts nearly 400 years of history and is recognized as one of Fukushima Prefecture’s most representative events, distinguished by the offering of Japan’s largest straw sandal (*waraji*). The sandal bearers include local volunteers from the Oyama Keishinkai and members of the local chamber of commerce, with local elementary school students participating as bearers of the children’s *waraji*.
The main route starts in the Oyama district on the northern peak of Mount Shinobu, proceeds counterclockwise through the area near [[:en:Fukushima Station|Fukushima Station]] East Exit, traverses the city center, and enters Mount Shinobu from the southern peak, culminating with an offering at {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine|qq=}} at the summit.
In terms of media coverage, the event is broadcast live annually by the local [[:en:Community radio|community radio]] station {{ill|FM Poco|ja|福島コミュニティ放送|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/FM Poco|qq=}}, available for free nationwide via online streaming.
A massive *waraji* measuring 12 meters in length, 1.2 meters in width, and weighing approximately 2 tons is carried during the event.
== Giant Waraji Procession Route == * Oyama Workshop (along [[:en:Japan National Route 13|National Route 13 (Japan)]]) * {{ill|Sato Construction Headquarters|ja|佐藤工業 (福島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sato Construction Headquarters|qq=}} (along {{ill|Fukushima Iizaka Line|ja|福島県道3号福島飯坂線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Iizaka Line|qq=}}) * {{ill|Fukushima Naritasan Fudoin|ja|福島成田山不動院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Naritasan Fudoin|qq=}} * [[:en:Soneda Station|Soneda Station]] / {{ill|MAX Fukushima|ja|曽根田ショッピングセンター|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/MAX Fukushima|qq=}} * [[:en:Fukushima Station|Fukushima Station]] East Exit * Fukushima Financial District (Brick Street) * {{ill|Fukushima Inari Shrine|ja|福島稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Inari Shrine|qq=}} (former guardian shrine of the Fukushima Castle town area) * Paseo Street Shopping District * Fukushima Fire Department * Entry to Mount Shinobu * {{ill|Kurōnuma Shrine|ja|黒沼神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurōnuma Shrine|qq=}} (guardian shrine of the former {{ill|Koyama Arai Village|ja|小山荒井村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyama Arai Village|qq=}} area) * {{ill|Fukushima Gokoku Shrine|ja|福島縣護國神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} * Mount Shinobu Guide Center * {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine|qq=}} (summit)
== Events == On the 10th, the "Akatsuki Mairi Fukuotoko Fukujyo Race" (*暁まいり福男福女競走*) is held, with participants competing along a route similar to the *waraji* procession.
== Access == On the day of the event, temporary free parking lots are set up at various locations around the southern peak of Mount Shinobu.
== External Links == * [Official Website of the Shinobu Sanzan Akatsuki Mairi (if available)]
* [http://www.f-kankou.jp/cgi-bin/f-kankou/asobu/page.cgi?id=70 信夫三山暁まいり] - 福島市観光コンベンション協会 * [https://www.ntt-east.co.jp/fukushima/fbrari/031/05.html イベント「信夫三山暁まいり」] - NTT東日本福島支店

revision.text:
{{otheruses|Shinobu Sanzan Akatsuki Mairi, a Shinto ritual held in February during the lunar New Year|Fukushima Waraji Festival, a summer event held in August|福島わらじまつり}}
'''Shinobu Sanzan Akatsuki Mairi''' (信夫三山暁まいり) is a {{ill|Shinto ritual|ja|神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto ritual|qq=}} held annually from [[:en:February 10|February 10]] to [[:en:February 11|February 11]], coinciding with the 14th day of the lunar New Year. It takes place on [[:en:Mount Shinobu|Mount Shinobu]], located in the center of [[:en:Fukushima (city)|Fukushima City]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]].
{{seealso|Fukushima Waraji Festival}}
== Overview == Mount Shinobu, adjacent to the urban area of Fukushima City, is venerated through [[:en:Mountain worship|mountain worship]] that honors its three peaks: Hayama, Haguroyama, and Kumano-yama. This festival boasts nearly 400 years of history and is recognized as one of Fukushima Prefecture’s most representative events, distinguished by the offering of Japan’s largest straw sandal (*waraji*). The sandal bearers include local volunteers from the Oyama Keishinkai and members of the local chamber of commerce, with local elementary school students participating as bearers of the children’s *waraji*.
The main route starts in the Oyama district on the northern peak of Mount Shinobu, proceeds counterclockwise through the area near [[:en:Fukushima Station|Fukushima Station]] East Exit, traverses the city center, and enters Mount Shinobu from the southern peak, culminating with an offering at {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine|qq=}} at the summit.
In terms of media coverage, the event is broadcast live annually by the local [[:en:Community radio|community radio]] station {{ill|FM Poco|ja|福島コミュニティ放送|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/FM Poco|qq=}}, available for free nationwide via online streaming.
A massive *waraji* measuring 12 meters in length, 1.2 meters in width, and weighing approximately 2 tons is carried during the event.
== Giant Waraji Procession Route == * Oyama Workshop (along [[:en:Japan National Route 13|National Route 13 (Japan)]]) * {{ill|Sato Construction Headquarters|ja|佐藤工業 (福島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sato Construction Headquarters|qq=}} (along {{ill|Fukushima Iizaka Line|ja|福島県道3号福島飯坂線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Iizaka Line|qq=}}) * {{ill|Fukushima Naritasan Fudoin|ja|福島成田山不動院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Naritasan Fudoin|qq=}} * [[:en:Soneda Station|Soneda Station]] / {{ill|MAX Fukushima|ja|曽根田ショッピングセンター|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/MAX Fukushima|qq=}} * [[:en:Fukushima Station|Fukushima Station]] East Exit * Fukushima Financial District (Brick Street) * {{ill|Fukushima Inari Shrine|ja|福島稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Inari Shrine|qq=}} (former guardian shrine of the Fukushima Castle town area) * Paseo Street Shopping District * Fukushima Fire Department * Entry to Mount Shinobu * {{ill|Kurōnuma Shrine|ja|黒沼神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurōnuma Shrine|qq=}} (guardian shrine of the former {{ill|Koyama Arai Village|ja|小山荒井村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyama Arai Village|qq=}} area) * {{ill|Fukushima Gokoku Shrine|ja|福島縣護國神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} * Mount Shinobu Guide Center * {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine|qq=}} (summit)
== Events == On the 10th, the "Akatsuki Mairi Fukuotoko Fukujyo Race" (*暁まいり福男福女競走*) is held, with participants competing along a route similar to the *waraji* procession.
== Access == On the day of the event, temporary free parking lots are set up at various locations around the southern peak of Mount Shinobu.
== External Links == * [Official Website of the Shinobu Sanzan Akatsuki Mairi (if available)]
* [http://www.f-kankou.jp/cgi-bin/f-kankou/asobu/page.cgi?id=70 信夫三山暁まいり] - 福島市観光コンベンション協会 * [https://www.ntt-east.co.jp/fukushima/fbrari/031/05.html イベント「信夫三山暁まいり」] - NTT東日本福島支店
{{translated page|ja|信夫三山暁まいり|version=101070461|comment=Imported full ja history}}

'''Shinobu Sanzan Akatsuki Mairi''' (信夫三山暁まいり) is a {{ill|Shinto ritual|ja|神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto ritual|qq=|1=Shinto ritual|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588671}} held annually from [[:en:February 10|February 10]] to [[:en:February 11|February 11]], coinciding with the 14th day of the lunar New Year. It takes place on [[:en:Mount Shinobu|Mount Shinobu]], located in the center of [[:en:Fukushima (city)|Fukushima City]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]].
{{seealso|Fukushima Waraji Festival}}
== Overview == Mount Shinobu, adjacent to the urban area of Fukushima City, is venerated through [[:en:Mountain worship|mountain worship]] that honors its three peaks: Hayama, Haguroyama, and Kumano-yama. This festival boasts nearly 400 years of history and is recognized as one of Fukushima Prefecture’s most representative events, distinguished by the offering of Japan’s largest straw sandal (*waraji*). The sandal bearers include local volunteers from the Oyama Keishinkai and members of the local chamber of commerce, with local elementary school students participating as bearers of the children’s *waraji*.
The main route starts in the Oyama district on the northern peak of Mount Shinobu, proceeds counterclockwise through the area near [[:en:Fukushima Station|Fukushima Station]] East Exit, traverses the city center, and enters Mount Shinobu from the southern peak, culminating with an offering at {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine|qq=}} at the summit.
In terms of media coverage, the event is broadcast live annually by the local [[:en:Community radio|community radio]] station {{ill|FM Poco|ja|福島コミュニティ放送|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/FM Poco|qq=|1=FM Poco|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11592995}}, available for free nationwide via online streaming.

This festival boasts nearly 400 years of history and is recognized as one of Fukushima Prefecture’s most representative events, distinguished by the offering of Japan’s largest straw sandal (*waraji*). The sandal bearers include local volunteers from the Oyama Keishinkai and members of the local chamber of commerce, with local elementary school students participating as bearers of the children’s *waraji*.
The main route starts in the Oyama district on the northern peak of Mount Shinobu, proceeds counterclockwise through the area near [[:en:Fukushima Station|Fukushima Station]] East Exit, traverses the city center, and enters Mount Shinobu from the southern peak, culminating with an offering at {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine|qq=}} at the summit.
In terms of media coverage, the event is broadcast live annually by the local [[:en:Community radio|community radio]] station {{ill|FM Poco|ja|福島コミュニティ放送|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/FM Poco|1=FM Poco|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11592995}}, available for free nationwide via online streaming.
A massive *waraji* measuring 12 meters in length, 1.2 meters in width, and weighing approximately 2 tons is carried during the event.
== Giant Waraji Procession Route == * Oyama Workshop (along [[:en:Japan National Route 13|National Route 13 (Japan)]]) * {{ill|Sato Construction Headquarters|ja|佐藤工業 (福島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sato Construction Headquarters|1=Sato Construction Headquarters|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11383943}} (along {{ill|Fukushima Iizaka Line|ja|福島県道3号福島飯坂線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Iizaka Line|1=Fukushima Prefectural Road Route 3|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11593646}}) * {{ill|Fukushima Naritasan Fudoin|ja|福島成田山不動院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Naritasan Fudoin|1=Fukushima Naritasan Fudoin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106613063}} * [[:en:Soneda Station|Soneda Station]] / {{ill|MAX Fukushima|ja|曽根田ショッピングセンター|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/MAX Fukushima|1=MAX Fukushima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11515347}} * [[:en:Fukushima Station|Fukushima Station]] East Exit * Fukushima Financial District (Brick Street) * {{ill|Fukushima Inari Shrine|ja|福島稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Inari Shrine|1=Fukushima Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11593701}} (former guardian shrine of the Fukushima Castle town area) * Paseo Street Shopping District * Fukushima Fire Department * Entry to Mount Shinobu * {{ill|Kurōnuma Shrine|ja|黒沼神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurōnuma Shrine|1=Kuronuma Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228379}} (guardian shrine of the former {{ill|Koyama Arai Village|ja|小山荒井村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyama Arai Village|1=Koyama Arai Village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18336725}} area) * {{ill|Fukushima Gokoku Shrine|ja|福島縣護國神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} * Mount Shinobu Guide Center * {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine|qq=}} (summit)
== Events == On the 10th, the "Akatsuki Mairi Fukuotoko Fukujyo Race" (*暁まいり福男福女競走*) is held, with participants competing along a route similar to the *waraji* procession.
== Access == On the day of the event, temporary free parking lots are set up at various locations around the southern peak of Mount Shinobu.
== External Links == * [Official Website of the Shinobu Sanzan Akatsuki Mairi (if available)]
* [http://www.f-kankou.jp/cgi-bin/f-kankou/asobu/page.cgi?id=70 信夫三山暁まいり] - 福島市観光コンベンション協会 * [https://www.ntt-east.co.jp/fukushima/fbrari/031/05.html イベント「信夫三山暁まいり」] - NTT東日本福島支店

== Giant Waraji Procession Route == * Oyama Workshop (along [[:en:Japan National Route 13|National Route 13 (Japan)]]) * {{ill|Sato Construction Headquarters|ja|佐藤工業 (福島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sato Construction Headquarters|1=Sato Construction Headquarters|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11383943}} (along {{ill|Fukushima Iizaka Line|ja|福島県道3号福島飯坂線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Iizaka Line|1=Fukushima Prefectural Road Route 3|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11593646}}) * {{ill|Fukushima Naritasan Fudoin|ja|福島成田山不動院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Naritasan Fudoin|1=Fukushima Naritasan Fudoin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106613063}} * [[:en:Soneda Station|Soneda Station]] / {{ill|MAX Fukushima|ja|曽根田ショッピングセンター|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/MAX Fukushima|1=MAX Fukushima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11515347}} * [[:en:Fukushima Station|Fukushima Station]] East Exit * Fukushima Financial District (Brick Street) * {{ill|Fukushima Inari Shrine|ja|福島稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Inari Shrine|1=Fukushima Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11593701}} (former guardian shrine of the Fukushima Castle town area) * Paseo Street Shopping District * Fukushima Fire Department * Entry to Mount Shinobu * {{ill|Kurōnuma Shrine|ja|黒沼神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurōnuma Shrine|1=Kuronuma Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228379}} (guardian shrine of the former {{ill|Koyama Arai Village|ja|小山荒井村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyama Arai Village|1=Koyama Arai Village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18336725}} area) * {{ill|Fukushima Gokoku Shrine|ja|福島縣護國神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} * Mount Shinobu Guide Center * {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine|qq=}} (summit)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:信夫三山暁まいり]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q20044802]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:22, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{otheruses|Shinobu Sanzan Akatsuki Mairi, a Shinto ritual held in February during the lunar New Year|Fukushima Waraji Festival, a summer event held in August|福島わらじまつり}}
'''Shinobu Sanzan Akatsuki Mairi''' (信夫三山暁まいり) is a {{ill|Shinto ritual|ja|神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto ritual|1=Shinto ritual|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588671}} held annually from [[:en:February 10|February 10]] to [[:en:February 11|February 11]], coinciding with the 14th day of the lunar New Year. It takes place on [[:en:Mount Shinobu|Mount Shinobu]], located in the center of [[:en:Fukushima (city)|Fukushima City]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]].
{{seealso|Fukushima Waraji Festival}}
== Overview == Mount Shinobu, adjacent to the urban area of Fukushima City, is venerated through [[:en:Mountain worship|mountain worship]] that honors its three peaks: Hayama, Haguroyama, and Kumano-yama. This festival boasts nearly 400 years of history and is recognized as one of Fukushima Prefecture’s most representative events, distinguished by the offering of Japan’s largest straw sandal (*waraji*). The sandal bearers include local volunteers from the Oyama Keishinkai and members of the local chamber of commerce, with local elementary school students participating as bearers of the children’s *waraji*.
The main route starts in the Oyama district on the northern peak of Mount Shinobu, proceeds counterclockwise through the area near [[:en:Fukushima Station|Fukushima Station]] East Exit, traverses the city center, and enters Mount Shinobu from the southern peak, culminating with an offering at {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine|qq=}} at the summit.
In terms of media coverage, the event is broadcast live annually by the local [[:en:Community radio|community radio]] station {{ill|FM Poco|ja|福島コミュニティ放送|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/FM Poco|1=FM Poco|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11592995}}, available for free nationwide via online streaming.
A massive *waraji* measuring 12 meters in length, 1.2 meters in width, and weighing approximately 2 tons is carried during the event.

== Giant Waraji Procession Route == * Oyama Workshop (along [[:en:Japan National Route 13|National Route 13 (Japan)]]) * {{ill|Sato Construction Headquarters|ja|佐藤工業 (福島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sato Construction Headquarters|1=Sato Construction Headquarters|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11383943}} (along {{ill|Fukushima Iizaka Line|ja|福島県道3号福島飯坂線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Iizaka Line|1=Fukushima Prefectural Road Route 3|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11593646}}) * {{ill|Fukushima Naritasan Fudoin|ja|福島成田山不動院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Naritasan Fudoin|1=Fukushima Naritasan Fudoin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106613063}} * [[:en:Soneda Station|Soneda Station]] / {{ill|MAX Fukushima|ja|曽根田ショッピングセンター|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/MAX Fukushima|1=MAX Fukushima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11515347}} * [[:en:Fukushima Station|Fukushima Station]] East Exit * Fukushima Financial District (Brick Street) * {{ill|Fukushima Inari Shrine|ja|福島稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Inari Shrine|1=Fukushima Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11593701}} (former guardian shrine of the Fukushima Castle town area) * Paseo Street Shopping District * Fukushima Fire Department * Entry to Mount Shinobu * {{ill|Kurōnuma Shrine|ja|黒沼神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurōnuma Shrine|1=Kuronuma Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228379}} (guardian shrine of the former {{ill|Koyama Arai Village|ja|小山荒井村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyama Arai Village|1=Koyama Arai Village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18336725}} area) * {{ill|Fukushima Gokoku Shrine|ja|福島縣護國神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} * Mount Shinobu Guide Center * {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine|qq=}} (summit)
== Events == On the 10th, the "Akatsuki Mairi Fukuotoko Fukujyo Race" (*暁まいり福男福女競走*) is held, with participants competing along a route similar to the *waraji* procession.
== Access == On the day of the event, temporary free parking lots are set up at various locations around the southern peak of Mount Shinobu.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:信夫三山暁まいり]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q20044802]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:22, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{otheruses|Shinobu Sanzan Akatsuki Mairi, a Shinto ritual held in February during the lunar New Year|Fukushima Waraji Festival, a summer event held in August|福島わらじまつり}}
'''Shinobu Sanzan Akatsuki Mairi''' (信夫三山暁まいり) is a {{ill|Shinto ritual|ja|神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto ritual|1=Shinto ritual|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588671}} held annually from [[:en:February 10|February 10]] to [[:en:February 11|February 11]], coinciding with the 14th day of the lunar New Year. It takes place on [[:en:Mount Shinobu|Mount Shinobu]], located in the center of [[:en:Fukushima (city)|Fukushima City]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]].
{{seealso|Fukushima Waraji Festival}}
== Overview == Mount Shinobu, adjacent to the urban area of Fukushima City, is venerated through [[:en:Mountain worship|mountain worship]] that honors its three peaks: Hayama, Haguroyama, and Kumano-yama. This festival boasts nearly 400 years of history and is recognized as one of Fukushima Prefecture’s most representative events, distinguished by the offering of Japan’s largest straw sandal (*waraji*). The sandal bearers include local volunteers from the Oyama Keishinkai and members of the local chamber of commerce, with local elementary school students participating as bearers of the children’s *waraji*.
The main route starts in the Oyama district on the northern peak of Mount Shinobu, proceeds counterclockwise through the area near [[:en:Fukushima Station|Fukushima Station]] East Exit, traverses the city center, and enters Mount Shinobu from the southern peak, culminating with an offering at {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine|qq=}} at the summit.
In terms of media coverage, the event is broadcast live annually by the local [[:en:Community radio|community radio]] station {{ill|FM Poco|ja|福島コミュニティ放送|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/FM Poco|1=FM Poco|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11592995}}, available for free nationwide via online streaming.
A massive *waraji* measuring 12 meters in length, 1.2 meters in width, and weighing approximately 2 tons is carried during the event.

== Giant Waraji Procession Route == * Oyama Workshop (along [[:en:Japan National Route 13|National Route 13 (Japan)]]) * {{ill|Sato Construction Headquarters|ja|佐藤工業 (福島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sato Construction Headquarters|1=Sato Construction Headquarters|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11383943}} (along {{ill|Fukushima Iizaka Line|ja|福島県道3号福島飯坂線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Iizaka Line|1=Fukushima Prefectural Road Route 3|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11593646}}) * {{ill|Fukushima Naritasan Fudoin|ja|福島成田山不動院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Naritasan Fudoin|1=Fukushima Naritasan Fudoin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106613063}} * [[:en:Soneda Station|Soneda Station]] / {{ill|MAX Fukushima|ja|曽根田ショッピングセンター|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/MAX Fukushima|1=MAX Fukushima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11515347}} * [[:en:Fukushima Station|Fukushima Station]] East Exit * Fukushima Financial District (Brick Street) * {{ill|Fukushima Inari Shrine|ja|福島稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Inari Shrine|1=Fukushima Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11593701}} (former guardian shrine of the Fukushima Castle town area) * Paseo Street Shopping District * Fukushima Fire Department * Entry to Mount Shinobu * {{ill|Kurōnuma Shrine|ja|黒沼神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurōnuma Shrine|1=Kuronuma Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228379}} (guardian shrine of the former {{ill|Koyama Arai Village|ja|小山荒井村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyama Arai Village|1=Koyama Arai Village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18336725}} area) * {{ill|Fukushima Gokoku Shrine|ja|福島縣護國神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} * Mount Shinobu Guide Center * {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine|qq=}} (summit)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:信夫三山暁まいり]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:信夫三山暁まいり]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:信夫三山暁まいり]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q20044802]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:22, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{otheruses|Shinobu Sanzan Akatsuki Mairi, a Shinto ritual held in February during the lunar New Year|Fukushima Waraji Festival, a summer event held in August|福島わらじまつり}}
'''Shinobu Sanzan Akatsuki Mairi''' (信夫三山暁まいり) is a {{ill|Shinto ritual|ja|神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto ritual|1=Shinto ritual|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588671}} held annually from [[:en:February 10|February 10]] to [[:en:February 11|February 11]], coinciding with the 14th day of the lunar New Year. It takes place on [[:en:Mount Shinobu|Mount Shinobu]], located in the center of [[:en:Fukushima (city)|Fukushima City]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]].
{{seealso|Fukushima Waraji Festival}}
== Overview == Mount Shinobu, adjacent to the urban area of Fukushima City, is venerated through [[:en:Mountain worship|mountain worship]] that honors its three peaks: Hayama, Haguroyama, and Kumano-yama. This festival boasts nearly 400 years of history and is recognized as one of Fukushima Prefecture’s most representative events, distinguished by the offering of Japan’s largest straw sandal (*waraji*). The sandal bearers include local volunteers from the Oyama Keishinkai and members of the local chamber of commerce, with local elementary school students participating as bearers of the children’s *waraji*.
The main route starts in the Oyama district on the northern peak of Mount Shinobu, proceeds counterclockwise through the area near [[:en:Fukushima Station|Fukushima Station]] East Exit, traverses the city center, and enters Mount Shinobu from the southern peak, culminating with an offering at {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine|qq=}} at the summit.
In terms of media coverage, the event is broadcast live annually by the local [[:en:Community radio|community radio]] station {{ill|FM Poco|ja|福島コミュニティ放送|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/FM Poco|1=FM Poco|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11592995}}, available for free nationwide via online streaming.
A massive *waraji* measuring 12 meters in length, 1.2 meters in width, and weighing approximately 2 tons is carried during the event.

== Events == On the 10th, the "Akatsuki Mairi Fukuotoko Fukujyo Race" (*暁まいり福男福女競走*) is held, with participants competing along a route similar to the *waraji* procession.
== Access == On the day of the event, temporary free parking lots are set up at various locations around the southern peak of Mount Shinobu.

== Giant Waraji Procession Route == * Oyama Workshop (along [[:en:Japan National Route 13|National Route 13 (Japan)]]) * {{ill|Sato Construction Headquarters|ja|佐藤工業 (福島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sato Construction Headquarters|1=Sato Construction Headquarters|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11383943}} (along {{ill|Fukushima Iizaka Line|ja|福島県道3号福島飯坂線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Iizaka Line|1=Fukushima Prefectural Road Route 3|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11593646}}) * {{ill|Fukushima Naritasan Fudoin|ja|福島成田山不動院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Naritasan Fudoin|1=Fukushima Naritasan Fudoin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106613063}} * [[:en:Soneda Station|Soneda Station]] / {{ill|MAX Fukushima|ja|曽根田ショッピングセンター|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/MAX Fukushima|1=MAX Fukushima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11515347}} * [[:en:Fukushima Station|Fukushima Station]] East Exit * Fukushima Financial District (Brick Street) * {{ill|Fukushima Inari Shrine|ja|福島稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Inari Shrine|1=Fukushima Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11593701}} (former guardian shrine of the Fukushima Castle town area) * Paseo Street Shopping District * Fukushima Fire Department * Entry to Mount Shinobu * {{ill|Kurōnuma Shrine|ja|黒沼神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurōnuma Shrine|1=Kuronuma Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228379}} (guardian shrine of the former {{ill|Koyama Arai Village|ja|小山荒井村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyama Arai Village|1=Koyama Arai Village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18336725}} area) * {{ill|Fukushima Gokoku Shrine|ja|福島縣護國神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} * Mount Shinobu Guide Center * {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine|qq=}} (summit)
