'''Shinobu Sanzan Akatsuki Mairi''' (信夫三山暁まいり) is a {{ill|Shinto ritual|ja|神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto ritual|1=Shinto ritual|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588671}} held annually from [[:en:February 10|February 10]] to [[:en:February 11|February 11]], coinciding with the 14th day of the lunar New Year. It takes place on [[:en:Mount Shinobu|Mount Shinobu]], located in the center of [[:en:Fukushima (city)|Fukushima City]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]].
{{seealso|Fukushima Waraji Festival}}
== Overview == Mount Shinobu, adjacent to the urban area of Fukushima City, is venerated through [[:en:Mountain worship|mountain worship]] that honors its three peaks: Hayama, Haguroyama, and Kumano-yama. This festival boasts nearly 400 years of history and is recognized as one of Fukushima Prefecture’s most representative events, distinguished by the offering of Japan’s largest straw sandal (*waraji*). The sandal bearers include local volunteers from the Oyama Keishinkai and members of the local chamber of commerce, with local elementary school students participating as bearers of the children’s *waraji*.
The main route starts in the Oyama district on the northern peak of Mount Shinobu, proceeds counterclockwise through the area near [[:en:Fukushima Station|Fukushima Station]] East Exit, traverses the city center, and enters Mount Shinobu from the southern peak, culminating with an offering at {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine|qq=}} at the summit.
In terms of media coverage, the event is broadcast live annually by the local [[:en:Community radio|community radio]] station {{ill|FM Poco|ja|福島コミュニティ放送|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/FM Poco|1=FM Poco|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11592995}}, available for free nationwide via online streaming.
A massive *waraji* measuring 12 meters in length, 1.2 meters in width, and weighing approximately 2 tons is carried during the event.

== Giant Waraji Procession Route == * Oyama Workshop (along [[:en:Japan National Route 13|National Route 13 (Japan)]]) * {{ill|Sato Construction Headquarters|ja|佐藤工業 (福島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sato Construction Headquarters|1=Sato Construction Headquarters|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11383943}} (along {{ill|Fukushima Iizaka Line|ja|福島県道3号福島飯坂線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Iizaka Line|1=Fukushima Prefectural Road Route 3|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11593646}}) * {{ill|Fukushima Naritasan Fudoin|ja|福島成田山不動院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Naritasan Fudoin|1=Fukushima Naritasan Fudoin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106613063}} * [[:en:Soneda Station|Soneda Station]] / {{ill|MAX Fukushima|ja|曽根田ショッピングセンター|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/MAX Fukushima|1=MAX Fukushima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11515347}} * [[:en:Fukushima Station|Fukushima Station]] East Exit * Fukushima Financial District (Brick Street) * {{ill|Fukushima Inari Shrine|ja|福島稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Inari Shrine|1=Fukushima Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11593701}} (former guardian shrine of the Fukushima Castle town area) * Paseo Street Shopping District * Fukushima Fire Department * Entry to Mount Shinobu * {{ill|Kurōnuma Shrine|ja|黒沼神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurōnuma Shrine|1=Kuronuma Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228379}} (guardian shrine of the former {{ill|Koyama Arai Village|ja|小山荒井村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyama Arai Village|1=Koyama Arai Village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18336725}} area) * {{ill|Fukushima Gokoku Shrine|ja|福島縣護國神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} * Mount Shinobu Guide Center * {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine|qq=}} (summit)
== Events == On the 10th, the "Akatsuki Mairi Fukuotoko Fukujyo Race" (*暁まいり福男福女競走*) is held, with participants competing along a route similar to the *waraji* procession.
== Access == On the day of the event, temporary free parking lots are set up at various locations around the southern peak of Mount Shinobu.
== External Links == * [Official Website of the Shinobu Sanzan Akatsuki Mairi (if available)]
* [http://www.f-kankou.jp/cgi-bin/f-kankou/asobu/page.cgi?id=70 信夫三山暁まいり] - 福島市観光コンベンション協会 * [https://www.ntt-east.co.jp/fukushima/fbrari/031/05.html イベント「信夫三山暁まいり」] - NTT東日本福島支店

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:信夫三山暁まいり]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:信夫三山暁まいり]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:信夫三山暁まいり]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q20044802]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:22, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{otheruses|Shinobu Sanzan Akatsuki Mairi, a Shinto ritual held in February during the lunar New Year|Fukushima Waraji Festival, a summer event held in August|福島わらじまつり}}
'''Shinobu Sanzan Akatsuki Mairi''' (信夫三山暁まいり) is a {{ill|Shinto ritual|ja|神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto ritual|1=Shinto ritual|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588671}} held annually from {{ill|February 10|en|February 10|WD=Q2325|lt=February 10}} to {{ill|February 11|en|February 11|WD=Q2335|lt=February 11}}, coinciding with the 14th day of the lunar New Year. It takes place on {{ill|Mount Shinobu|en|Mount Shinobu|WD=Q6923612|lt=Mount Shinobu}}, located in the center of {{ill|Fukushima (city)|en|Fukushima (city)|WD=Q161176|lt=Fukushima City}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture}}.
{{seealso|Fukushima Waraji Festival}}
== Overview == Mount Shinobu, adjacent to the urban area of Fukushima City, is venerated through {{ill|Mountain worship|en|Mountain worship|WD=Q93697439|lt=mountain worship}} that honors its three peaks: Hayama, Haguroyama, and Kumano-yama. This festival boasts nearly 400 years of history and is recognized as one of Fukushima Prefecture’s most representative events, distinguished by the offering of Japan’s largest straw sandal (*waraji*). The sandal bearers include local volunteers from the Oyama Keishinkai and members of the local chamber of commerce, with local elementary school students participating as bearers of the children’s *waraji*.
The main route starts in the Oyama district on the northern peak of Mount Shinobu, proceeds counterclockwise through the area near {{ill|Fukushima Station|en|Fukushima Station|WD=Q416034|lt=Fukushima Station}} East Exit, traverses the city center, and enters Mount Shinobu from the southern peak, culminating with an offering at {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine|qq=}} at the summit.
In terms of media coverage, the event is broadcast live annually by the local {{ill|Community radio|en|Community radio|WD=Q1179112|lt=community radio}} station {{ill|FM Poco|ja|福島コミュニティ放送|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/FM Poco|1=FM Poco|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11592995}}, available for free nationwide via online streaming.
A massive *waraji* measuring 12 meters in length, 1.2 meters in width, and weighing approximately 2 tons is carried during the event.
== Giant Waraji Procession Route == * Oyama Workshop (along {{ill|Japan National Route 13|en|Japan National Route 13|WD=Q1056193|lt=National Route 13 (Japan)}}) * {{ill|Sato Construction Headquarters|ja|佐藤工業 (福島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sato Construction Headquarters|1=Sato Construction Headquarters|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11383943}} (along {{ill|Fukushima Iizaka Line|ja|福島県道3号福島飯坂線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Iizaka Line|1=Fukushima Prefectural Road Route 3|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11593646}}) * {{ill|Fukushima Naritasan Fudoin|ja|福島成田山不動院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Naritasan Fudoin|1=Fukushima Naritasan Fudoin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106613063}} * {{ill|Soneda Station|en|Soneda Station|WD=Q7560926|lt=Soneda Station}} / {{ill|MAX Fukushima|ja|曽根田ショッピングセンター|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/MAX Fukushima|1=MAX Fukushima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11515347}} * {{ill|Fukushima Station|en|Fukushima Station|WD=Q416034|lt=Fukushima Station}} East Exit * Fukushima Financial District (Brick Street) * {{ill|Fukushima Inari Shrine|ja|福島稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Inari Shrine|1=Fukushima Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11593701}} (former guardian shrine of the Fukushima Castle town area) * Paseo Street Shopping District * Fukushima Fire Department * Entry to Mount Shinobu * {{ill|Kurōnuma Shrine|ja|黒沼神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurōnuma Shrine|1=Kuronuma Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228379}} (guardian shrine of the former {{ill|Koyama Arai Village|ja|小山荒井村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyama Arai Village|1=Koyama Arai Village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18336725}} area) * {{ill|Fukushima Gokoku Shrine|ja|福島縣護國神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} * Mount Shinobu Guide Center * {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine|qq=}} (summit)
== Events == On the 10th, the "Akatsuki Mairi Fukuotoko Fukujyo Race" (*暁まいり福男福女競走*) is held, with participants competing along a route similar to the *waraji* procession.
== Access == On the day of the event, temporary free parking lots are set up at various locations around the southern peak of Mount Shinobu.
== External Links == * [Official Website of the Shinobu Sanzan Akatsuki Mairi (if available)]
* [http://www.f-kankou.jp/cgi-bin/f-kankou/asobu/page.cgi?id=70 信夫三山暁まいり] - 福島市観光コンベンション協会 * [https://www.ntt-east.co.jp/fukushima/fbrari/031/05.html イベント「信夫三山暁まいり」] - NTT東日本福島支店

The main route starts in the Oyama district on the northern peak of Mount Shinobu, proceeds counterclockwise through the area near {{ill|Fukushima Station|en|Fukushima Station|WD=Q416034|lt=Fukushima Station}} East Exit, traverses the city center, and enters Mount Shinobu from the southern peak, culminating with an offering at {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine|qq=}} at the summit.
In terms of media coverage, the event is broadcast live annually by the local {{ill|Community radio|en|Community radio|WD=Q1179112|lt=community radio}} station {{ill|FM Poco|ja|福島コミュニティ放送|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/FM Poco|1=FM Poco|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11592995}}, available for free nationwide via online streaming.
A massive *waraji* measuring 12 meters in length, 1.2 meters in width, and weighing approximately 2 tons is carried during the event.
== Giant Waraji Procession Route == * Oyama Workshop (along {{ill|Japan National Route 13|en|Japan National Route 13|WD=Q1056193|lt=National Route 13 (Japan)}}) * {{ill|Sato Construction Headquarters|ja|佐藤工業 (福島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sato Construction Headquarters|1=Sato Construction Headquarters|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11383943}} (along {{ill|Fukushima Iizaka Line|ja|福島県道3号福島飯坂線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Iizaka Line|1=Fukushima Prefectural Road Route 3|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11593646}}) * {{ill|Fukushima Naritasan Fudoin|ja|福島成田山不動院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Naritasan Fudoin|1=Fukushima Naritasan Fudoin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106613063}} * {{ill|Soneda Station|en|Soneda Station|WD=Q7560926|lt=Soneda Station}} / {{ill|MAX Fukushima|ja|曽根田ショッピングセンター|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/MAX Fukushima|1=MAX Fukushima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11515347}} * {{ill|Fukushima Station|en|Fukushima Station|WD=Q416034|lt=Fukushima Station}} East Exit * Fukushima Financial District (Brick Street) * {{ill|Fukushima Inari Shrine|ja|福島稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Inari Shrine|1=Fukushima Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11593701}} (former guardian shrine of the Fukushima Castle town area) * Paseo Street Shopping District * Fukushima Fire Department * Entry to Mount Shinobu * {{ill|Kurōnuma Shrine|ja|黒沼神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurōnuma Shrine|1=Kuronuma Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228379}} (guardian shrine of the former {{ill|Koyama Arai Village|ja|小山荒井村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyama Arai Village|1=Koyama Arai Village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18336725}} area) * {{ill|Fukushima Gokoku Shrine|ja|福島縣護國神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} * Mount Shinobu Guide Center * {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine|qq=}} (summit)
== Events == On the 10th, the "Akatsuki Mairi Fukuotoko Fukujyo Race" (*暁まいり福男福女競走*) is held, with participants competing along a route similar to the *waraji* procession.
== Access == On the day of the event, temporary free parking lots are set up at various locations around the southern peak of Mount Shinobu.
== External Links == * [Official Website of the Shinobu Sanzan Akatsuki Mairi (if available)]
* [http://www.f-kankou.jp/cgi-bin/f-kankou/asobu/page.cgi?id=70 信夫三山暁まいり] - 福島市観光コンベンション協会 * [https://www.ntt-east.co.jp/fukushima/fbrari/031/05.html イベント「信夫三山暁まいり」] - NTT東日本福島支店

{{otheruses|Shinobu Sanzan Akatsuki Mairi, a Shinto ritual held in February during the lunar New Year|Fukushima Waraji Festival, a summer event held in August|福島わらじまつり}}
'''Shinobu Sanzan Akatsuki Mairi''' (信夫三山暁まいり) is a {{ill|Shinto ritual|ja|神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto ritual|1=Shinto ritual|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588671}} held annually from {{ill|February 10|en|February 10|WD=Q2325|lt=February 10|1=February 10}} to {{ill|February 11|en|February 11|WD=Q2335|lt=February 11|1=February 11}}, coinciding with the 14th day of the lunar New Year. It takes place on {{ill|Mount Shinobu|en|Mount Shinobu|WD=Q6923612|lt=Mount Shinobu|1=Mount Shinobu}}, located in the center of {{ill|Fukushima (city)|en|Fukushima (city)|WD=Q161176|lt=Fukushima City|1=Fukushima (city)}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}.
{{seealso|Fukushima Waraji Festival}}
== Overview == Mount Shinobu, adjacent to the urban area of Fukushima City, is venerated through {{ill|Mountain worship|en|Mountain worship|WD=Q93697439|lt=mountain worship|1=Mountain worship}} that honors its three peaks: Hayama, Haguroyama, and Kumano-yama. This festival boasts nearly 400 years of history and is recognized as one of Fukushima Prefecture’s most representative events, distinguished by the offering of Japan’s largest straw sandal (*waraji*). The sandal bearers include local volunteers from the Oyama Keishinkai and members of the local chamber of commerce, with local elementary school students participating as bearers of the children’s *waraji*.
The main route starts in the Oyama district on the northern peak of Mount Shinobu, proceeds counterclockwise through the area near {{ill|Fukushima Station|en|Fukushima Station|WD=Q416034|lt=Fukushima Station|1=Fukushima Station}} East Exit, traverses the city center, and enters Mount Shinobu from the southern peak, culminating with an offering at {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine|qq=}} at the summit.
In terms of media coverage, the event is broadcast live annually by the local {{ill|Community radio|en|Community radio|WD=Q1179112|lt=community radio|1=Community radio}} station {{ill|FM Poco|ja|福島コミュニティ放送|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/FM Poco|1=Q11592995|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11592995}}, available for free nationwide via online streaming.
A massive *waraji* measuring 12 meters in length, 1.2 meters in width, and weighing approximately 2 tons is carried during the event.

Guide Center * {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine|qq=}} (summit)
== Events == On the 10th, the "Akatsuki Mairi Fukuotoko Fukujyo Race" (*暁まいり福男福女競走*) is held, with participants competing along a route similar to the *waraji* procession.
== Access == On the day of the event, temporary free parking lots are set up at various locations around the southern peak of Mount Shinobu.
